programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A woman seemingly dies as the result of a shark attack, but inquiries prove she had already been shot. As the investigation proceeds, it transpires the victim worked for a private investigation agency that specialised in entrapping married men suspected of infidelity - and a member of the CSI team becomes a leading suspect in her death. David Caruso stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team becomes involved in an international incident when the body of an American petty officer is found by an estate agent in a bungalow that has been repossessed. An indentation on the victim's skull leads the agents to believe he was killed on a British ship, but collecting evidence from the vessel proves challenging. Guest starring Tracy Middendorf, Daniel Gillies and Kristen Ariza

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A man who stole a car and ran down and killed one of his pursuers before escaping is identified as Walton Flynn, an ex-marine now working for a private security firm in Iraq. Sam and Callen learn that the businessman he had been hired to protect had been murdered and that the people chasing him were hired by his employers to dish out their own form of justice. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble hitches a ride with a local clergyman, but finds himself in jeopardy when the cleric is killed by a sniper's bullet.

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Logan and Briscoe investigate the death of a policeman in a shoot-out, but find his fellow officers strangely reluctant to answer questions - and begin to suspect that they may have deliberately failed to provide back-up. Drama, starring Chris Noth

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are called to the scene of a car accident in which a petty officer was killed. They discover a bullet wound in the body, as well as another victim at the bottom of a nearby ravine - a woman who was beaten to death. Ducky conducts an autopsy and makes a discovery that sets the agents off in search of a missing infant. Meanwhile, Tony is distracted by developments in his relationship with Jeanne

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The investigators discover probationary agent Ned Dorneget has been bugged by Harper Dearing, the man behind a spate of arson attacks on US Navy vessels. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A jet-skier crashes on to the shore and is discovered to be dead, having been shot through the chest. Sam and Callen visit the victim's apartment, where they find the body of a woman on the floor

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Lt Col Mann arrives to assist with inquiries into the murder of a marine - a case that becomes personal for Gibbs when he learns one of the witnesses is his ex-wife Stephanie. Starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the disappearance of photographer Martin Roe, who was due to testify at an army first lieutenant's court-martial after he captured a picture of what appeared to be a soldier killing an unarmed civilian. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Vance charges DiNozzo with a special assignment to go to France, locate Admiral Kendall's wayward daughter Amanda and bring her home. Arriving in Marseille, Tony immediately heads to the local NCIS office, only to find five dead agents. After eventually tracking down Amanda, the pair embark on a rollercoaster ride to the relative safety of the nearest NCIS office in Spain. Michael Weatherly stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs travels to his home town after hearing news of his father's death, while the team investigates whether a fire on a US Navy ship was accidental or planned. The vessel served as prison to several members of the Brotherhood of Doubt terrorist group, whom Gibbs was responsible for catching. After the blaze, two of those members are missing. programme.desc: Part one of two. When a doctor testifying against him in a malpractice suit is poisoned, Jesse is arrested and charged with murder, so Dr Sloan persuades old friend and attorney Ben Matlock to defend his colleague. Andy Griffith guest stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ben Matlock finds a trail of evidence that suggests the killers have struck before, framing more than one innocent person for murder, and sets out to catch them this time round. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Andy Griffith

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Old friends Dr Sloan and private investigator Joe Mannix are thrown together to unravel a case of corruption linked to an unsolved murder. Guest starring Mike Connors as the veteran TV detective

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries
programme.desc: Examining strange happenings, including a news producer, who finds out the truth behind his adoption, and a popular theatre student, who goes missing without a trace

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries
programme.desc: The case of a murder victim who seemingly possessed a co-worker so she could name her killer. Plus, the story of a rare white buffalo calf

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries
programme.desc: The case of a woman who was trampled to death by a horse is re-examined to determine if it was an accident or murder. Plus, the story of a man trying to find his father and son

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries
programme.desc: Examining strange happenings, including a wave of UFO sightings, which turn nasty for one local resident. Plus, an heiress and eccentric dog breeder goes missing

programme.title: Matlock
programme.desc: Part one of two. A coin dealer is charged with the murder of his thieving employee

programme.title: Matlock
programme.desc: Part two of two. A coin dealer is charged with murdering an employee who was stealing from him

programme.title: Matlock
programme.desc: A man with learning difficulties is charged with murder, but the victim's father believes he is innocent and begs Matlock to represent him

programme.title: Matlock
programme.desc: The lawyer gets hit by a car but is determined to return to work, despite the protestations of the nurse looking after him

programme.title: Matlock
programme.desc: The attorney gets involved in the case of a man charged with killing the leader of a cult

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Danny and Aiden investigate the case of employees of a Brooklyn cafe found dead with plastic bags over their heads. As events unfold, a discovery is made about the type of duct tape used on the victims which could help track down the killer. Meanwhile, Mac and Stella set out to locate the body of a man whose severed lower leg has turned up in an alleyway

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Criminals kidnap a bank employee's baby to force her to provide them with inside information on how to commit a robbery - but two are killed in the ensuing raid and the third escapes without revealing the youngster's location. The team immediately mounts a search, but work is hampered by relentless rain and an unexpected terrorist response drill

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Aiden try to solve the mystery of a stockbroker's death by analysing a cryptic message on his computer. Meanwhile, Stella investigates the case of a pregnant counsellor whose body has been found near the church where she worked. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Vanessa Ferlito and Melina Kanakaredes

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate the fatal shooting of a mounted police officer - but the only evidence is a bullet lodged in his horse, presenting them with a dilemma. Aiden takes on the case of a dead youth discovered with severe facial burns, and soon finds herself trying to crack an illegal betting operation

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate a sleepwalker accused of killing a woman whose body she was found beside - but the blood on her clothing and splinters in her hands are not necessarily proof of guilt. Meanwhile, Danny and Aiden look into the death of a professional pickpocket whose apartment reveals the extent of his activities. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: The Bill

Her frail health makes Anna and Gino decide to postpone telling their families about their relationship, but Carla becomes suspicious after finding contraception hidden in Anna's room

programme.title: Peak Practice

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth invites the new vicar round for tea - but despite her meticulous planning, the afternoon does not go as planned. Sitcom about a super-snob intent on climbing the social ladder, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Jeremy Gittins

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: A hang-gliding enthusiast crashes down in the village, mistakenly believing that he's landed in France, thus breaking the long-distance record. The gang tries to compensate for his disappointment by making him the best at something else - identifying a strange cat creature lurking in the woods. Peter Sallis, Frank Thornton and Brian Murphy star

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The locals are unhappy when an ostentatious American opens a golf club near Kembleford - only for a body to turn up in the reeds. Whodunit, with Mark Williams

programme.title: Frankie Drake Mysteries

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sasha is forced to work with her ex-husband Ned Hancock when their teams investigate the death of film critic Oliver Houghton, whose body was discovered floating in the Thames. UCOS delves into the victim's past and explores his link to the murder of a conceptual artist 20 years previously, while Hancock's knowledge of London's hidden past proves invaluable when a piece of occult footage from the 1970s plunges the investigators into the macabre world of human sacrifice. Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson star, with a guest appearance by Barnaby Kay

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Team members are posted undercover at the heart of the talks during the World Trade Summit for Africa, where they unearth an assassination plot which they must risk their lives to quash. programme.desc: Armed raiders rob wealthy guests at the restaurant, leaving Crabbe no option but to shut up shop. To make matters worse, hotshot DS Stringer is put in charge of the case, and soon finds himself unpopular with the staff by insulting Margaret and victimising Gary. Starring Richard Griffiths and Samantha Janus

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: The return of an old enemy leads to a series of problems, beginning with the loss of Sharpe's hard-earned captaincy, and culminating in a race against time to rescue his lover Teresa and their baby from the beleaguered city of Badajoz before Wellesley's forces attack. Military adventure, starring Sean Bean and Pete Postlethwaite

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: The team members set out to prevent a gangster's firearms deal, an act which Alex believes will prevent her being shot in 2008. To obtain evidence, they have to bring the suspect's boyfriend on side, taking him to a gay club where he can see his lover is not the man he thinks he is. Ray is forced to use all his macho charm to seduce the villain in front of witnesses. Drama starring Philip Glenister and Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Gene questions an old adversary about a robbery, and when it is revealed the crook has had to give up his life of crime, the detective worries that he too might be past his best. However, an interview with a murder suspect puts Alex in mortal danger - and leaves Gene drawing on his experience in an attempt to save her. Philip Glenister and Keeley Hawes star

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: Bacchus and Gently arrive in a small town to investigate the apparent suicide of a mill manager who has been found hanged. However, the detectives begin to suspect foul play as they discover money has gone missing and the victim's widow reveals he was having an affair. Starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The boss of Brokenwood agricultural show dies after a ride on the Ghost Train, and the investigation reveals his suspected heart attack was something far more sinister

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The fresh corpse of a professor is revealed in place of the cadaver during Dr Mac's anatomy lecture. Now estranged in their private lives, both Phryne and Jack constantly find themselves stepping around each other to solve the case and also determine the whereabouts of a valuable manuscript. Australian crime drama, starring Essie Davis and Nathan Page

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: A shocking incident on a suburban street brings back painful memories for Jack, forcing him to revisit his past. A member of the victim's family seeks justice of their own.

programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Glamorous opera singer Eleanor Samson becomes the prime suspect in a murder case after her estranged husband's lover is found in the burnt-out shell of a boat. Taggart's assistant Peter Livingstone believes Eleanor is guilty, but the grizzled detective's instincts suggest otherwise. Crime drama, guest starring Isla Blair with Mark McManus and Neil Duncan

programme.desc: Hugh finds an unlikely test subject for his revolutionary new artificial heart. Starring Rodger Corser, Nicole da Silva and Ryan Johnson

Zain's father is arrested on suspicion of burglary, but it turns out he is on a more high-minded mission on behalf of a friend. After a night of passion, Gina discovers her new conquest is a married man

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jack becomes impatient for a result in the undercover operation to trap drug dealer Jose Alvarez, and Zain panics when he's pressed to set up a sting which would bring Kristen Shaw down as well. Roger and Dan deal with a troublesome family who intend to spend their lottery win on one big party and Emma is left holding the baby, while James Tennant prepares for his court appearance by getting blind drunk

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: With the Serious Organised Crime Agency muscling in on his case, Jack orders Zain to take a break and brings Kristen Shaw in for questioning, but he's forced to let her go when she refuses to name the big boys in the Colombian drugs operation. Dan is sent home when Gina learns his steroid use caused him to lose control in the boxing match, leaving his opponent struggling for life. Lottery winners the Hendersons provide further proof money can't buy happiness

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Stuart and Terry are surprised to discover ex-colleague DC Jo Masters working undercover when they investigate a counterfeit satellite navigation systems operation, and learn that they have stumbled upon a much bigger crime involving ruthless gun-runners. Honey and Leela sort out a scuffle at a funeral involving feuding brothers and end up falling for the same smooth-talking Irishman

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Four-part adaptation of Charlotte Bronte's novel about an orphan girl who struggles to survive the harsh treatment of a boarding school to embark on a career as a governess. Jane takes up her first position at the home of the alluring and unpredictable Edward Rochester, under the watchful eye of laundrywoman Grace Poole. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Jane begins to wonder about the chemistry between herself and Rochester, who disappoints her when he leaves without warning. However, he returns with a houseful of distinguished guests, including the beautiful Blanche Ingram and her opinionated mother. Things turn sour when a mysterious visitor arrives. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: After fulfilling her dying aunt's last wishes, Jane returns to Thornfield Hall - only to find Mr Rochester is set to wed Blanche. However, after she confesses her love, he shares his true feelings and asks for her hand in marriage instead. While all seems well at first, a ghostly figure begins to torment Jane as the wedding day draws near. Drama, with Ruth Wilson and Toby Stephens

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: A clergyman and his two sisters take a heartbroken Jane under their wing after finding her alone and starving on the moors. The governess embarks on a new life as a parish teacher, but thinks she hears Rochester call out her name and feels compelled to return to Thornfield - where the secrets of the North Tower are finally revealed. Starring Ruth Wilson and Toby Stephens

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Tide of Life
programme.desc: Gillian Kearney stars in the feature-length period drama following the fortunes of housekeeper Emily Kennedy and her relationships with three very different men. At first she is content working for the McGilby family while her father is away at sea, but finds her life thrown into turmoil by the death of one of her employers. With John Bowler and Ray Stevenson

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Barbara faces her most challenging case so far when she treats a first-time mother, only for the birth not to go according to plan. A new nurse arrives and soon makes an impression on Sister Evangelina, while Sister Julienne is worried about the increasing financial pressure on Nonnatus House, but doesn't bank on a mystery benefactor coming to the rescue. Jenny Agutter and Pam Ferris star, with Linda Bassett (East Is East) joining the cast

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: A desperately poor Fantine is forced to flee Paris in search of work, and is left contemplating a terrible decision that could ensure her daughter enjoys a brighter future. Her fate takes an even darker turn as she struggles to meet the increasingly outrageous demands of the Thenardiers. Valjean settles into his new life as a prosperous factory owner, but his newfound happiness is threatened when Javert is appointed as the town's new police inspector, and remains as determined as ever to expose him

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Anne Boleyn summons Cromwell, anxious to know whether she has an ally in him, and he witnesses her venting her frustrations on her ladies-in-waiting. The king starts to take notice of Cromwell and comes to rely on the lawyer's advice, but then news arrives from York that Cardinal Wolsey has been arrested by Harry Percy, a vengeful act for denying his betrothal to Anne eight years earlier. Historical drama adapted from Hilary Mantel's Booker prize-winning novels Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies, starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Claire Foy and Jonathan Pryce

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: DI Pat North is assigned to look into a case that may clear a man convicted of murder eight years ago, and finds that DS Walker was in charge of the original inquiry. Drama, starring Kate Buffery, David Hayman and James Wilby

programme.title: Wuthering Heights
programme.desc: First in a two-part adaptation of Emily Bronte's classic tale. In 1815, Mr Earnshaw returns home from Liverpool with orphan Heathcliff, who befriends his daughter Cathy but is resented by his son Hindley. Upon his father's death 10 years later, Hindley inherits Wuthering Heights and forces Heathcliff to live in the stables, while Cathy must choose between a life of comfort and her love for Heathcliff. Tom Hardy, Charlotte Riley, Andrew Lincoln and Sarah Lancashire star

programme.desc: Edmund and Nora prepare to launch their app, and Wally's dinner date goes terribly awry

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook heads to Snowdonia to hear the real-life Romeo and Juliet story of Vincenzo Algieri and Betty Evans - two star-crossed lovers who, despite being on opposite sides of the Second World War, found love together against all the odds. From different cultures and speaking different languages, Vincenzo and Betty bonded over food - traditional Italian dishes the family still treasures to this day. Grandson Huw has some clues of the tale the lies within his family history, but hopes Michela's specialist knowledge can help fill in some of the blanks

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Liverpool v West Ham United and Manchester United v Arsenal

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Wales v Scotland (Kick-off 2.15pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from both teams' fifth and final match of the championship, held at Parc y Scarlets. The Welsh have failed to repeat their heroics of last year when they won the Grand Slam, losing three of their four contests. Meanwhile, Scotland started their campaign with two defeats, but then won in Italy before ending France's hope of a Grand Slam with a shock 28-17 triumph at Murrayfield. With analysis from Jeremy Guscott and Andy Nicol, and commentary by Andrew Cotter, Jonathan Davies and Chris Paterson

programme.title: Final Score

John Humphrys presents

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing

The stakes could not be higher because not only do the Strictly stars have to impress the judges Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse, but they also have to impress the viewers at home, who get the chance to vote for their favourites for the very first time this series

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Ireland. John Inverdale presents second-half coverage of the final match of the championship, which comes from Stade de France. Tonight's hosts looked in a strong position to win the Grand Slam after victories over England and Wales, but they then suffered a shock defeat at the hands of Scotland, though they are still in with a chance of securing the title. However, Ireland also go into this match in contention and are unbeaten in their last three Six Nations matches here. With analysis from Martin Johnson and Paul O'Connell, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Brian Moore and Jamie Heaslip

programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Kiwi-born Felicity stayed in the UK to raise her daughter, but now wants to relocate to Australia. However, daughter Lucy says she doesn't want to go, so the pair sample a week 'Down Under'. At the end of the trial, Lucy has to vote on where her future lies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow projects to make family homes out of a three-bed semi in Stoke-on-Trent, a split-level maisonette in Brixton, and a detached property in Surrey

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits Dundee, home of the Beano and the Dandy, to see how its comics tradition inspired a new version of the Bible. With music by Barbara Dickson and Cliff Richard

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Live Women's FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Everton v Manchester City (Kick-off 2.30pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from the final at Wembley Stadium. Everton are looking to lift the trophy for the first time since the 2009/10 season and have impressed since the competition resumed with victories over Chelsea and Everton. The Citizens have overcome Leicester City and Arsenal since the restart and will now have high hopes of winning the trophy for the third successive season

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design a garden for a family of seven in Stamford, Lincolnshire, who have just moved from rural Rutland and miss the countryside. With five noisy children, the parents want a peaceful garden that they can call their own, but the awkward plot gives the presenters a huge challenge, despite a budget of £4,500

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the sixth annual event, the team heads out across the UK to lead rambles and meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. Matt Baker is in the Peak District to take on challenge with 16-year-old Harvey, who was supported by bereavement counselling after his brother was killed, while Steve Brown is inspired to attempt scaling Wales' Pen y Fan mountain in his wheelchair after meeting 5-year-old Emerson, a sport-lover with a spinal-cord injury

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from the first two live weekends. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel-Horwood and Motsi Mabuse decide who will be the first pair to leave the contest. With music by Sam Smith

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness introduces Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff to his latest creation - an all-electric, all-terrain ice-cream van for the 21st century. Elsewhere, the team tests the battery-powered Honda E, Vauxhall Corsa-e and Mini Electric with a race around a deserted Alton Towers

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: Confronted about his affair, pressure mounts on the home front for Peter, while the party chairman asks him about his ambitions. Is he after the top job? Rochelle decides to pick up where Charmian left off

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, introducing highlights of Manchester United v Arsenal at Old Trafford and Tottenham Hotspur v Brighton & Hove Albion at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Plus, action from Aston Villa v Southampton at Villa Park and Newcastle United v Everton at St James' Park

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the final round of fixtures in the annual international rugby union tournament, featuring France v Ireland, Wales v Scotland and Italy v England

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Crewmates Adam and Dan treat a patient who has fallen down a flight of stairs in a casino, while Rachel and Stuart respond to a call when a child falls from a bunk bed. As the shift continues, a distressing call from a patient threatening to jump into the river takes priority, and the call handler must try to keep the patient talking until help can arrive

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: A tenant making more than £100,000 unlawfully sub-letting his council flat, and a criminal gang running a multimillion-pound diesel fraud

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours the skips at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey for things to restore and sell for a profit. Items include a pair of leather wallets and a rocking chair

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Fine art conservator Lucia Scalisi restores a treasured painting by celebrated artist CJ Frost, while furniture restorer Will Kirk sets to work on an antique sewing table. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher and David Burville repair a 130-year-old automaton tiger, and fountain pen restorer Laurence Oldfield tackles the restoration of a vintage Wyvern fountain pen

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Three people who have never met before must work as a team and answer questions correctly in a bid to win a share of a £20,000 cash prize. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques expert Caroline Hawley, trader Gary Barton and furniture designer Nessa Doran O'Reilly head to Whitchurch to find bargains they can turn into profitfor Children in Need

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Jan's pregnancy threatens to drive a wedge between George and Fiona, while demand for paintings by Dennis is outstripping supply, so Shay comes up with an ingenious solution

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the eight remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room for lingerie and sleepwear week. To test the sewers' handling skills, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young kick off the pattern challenge with a boned basque. The task requires sewing together small pattern pieces for a precise fit and creating a tricky hook and eye fastening. Next, in the transformation challenge, the sewers transform pyjamas and nightwear into a summer dress, before finally being tasked to create a two-piece female sleep set which has to incorporates lace somewhere in their design

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using new 3D technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott explore the Italian city of Florence, and with the help of historian Ross King and the team, they discover the hidden secrets of Florence's great cathedral and its architectural genius. The pair also learn more about the Medici's fraught relationship with Michelangelo, as he fell from favour when he supported a rebellion against his own masters, and visit the world-famous Uffizi - including the Botticelli room

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Culzean Castle on the west coast of Scotland in Ayrshire, experts Anita Manning and Natasha Raskin Sharp are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. A watercolour with a local connection catches Anita's eye, while Natasha is drawn to a gorgeous handwoven blanket. Paul Martin explores a hidden part of Culzean's history, uncovering the tales of smugglers working the coastline in the caves beneath the castle walls

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jayde Adams, Josie d'Arby, Rufus Hound and Scott Mills test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games. This edition sees the week's overall winner crowned

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal presents analysis of the couples' progress as they prepare for tomorrow night's show. Including reports from the studio rehearsals and interviews with celebrity fans

programme.title: Simply Nigella
programme.desc: Food writer Nigella Lawson examines how different days require certain types of food, sharing her favourite menu to help get that balance right in the kitchen. She prepares a light salad of salmon, avocado, watercress and pumpkin seeds, and a chicken shawarma with an array of spices, sauces and flatbreads - a dish inspired by a spit-roasted Lebanese kebab that is perfect for feeding a crowd. Nigella also creates two sweet treats, a glossy liquorice and blackcurrant chocolate cake, and chocolate chip cookie dough pots served with ice-cream or creme fraiche

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox is joined by comedians Jo Brand and Andi Osho, actor and comedian Adrian Edmondson and singer Will Young, who share their favourite books as well as revealing the books that remind them of a particular person and their recommended non-fiction reads. They all discuss this week's book group picks - This Lovely City by Louise Hare and Will's own book, To Be a Gay Man

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: In a Halloween-themed programme, Gillian Burke shows off the amazing hydraulic power of spiders' legs. Iolo Williams has an exclusive update on the remarkable story of a giant vulture that set up home in Derbyshire, Megan McCubbin and Chris Packham look back at the mouse experiment and their friendly foxes, and Michaela Strachan catches up on the highs and lows of the Scottish seal colony

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don harvests his pumpkins at Longmeadow, plants bulbs in a pot and has plenty of jobs for everyone to be getting on with over the coming weeks. Frances Tophill travels to Winkworth Arboretum in Surrey to celebrate the season and to explain the science behind the golden and red tones of autumn leaves. The team take inspiration from RHS Harlow Carr and take a look at their winter borders and the plants which will add interest and drama to gardens over the coming months

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools chats to Pulp frontman Jarvis Cocker about his musical journey, illustrated by a selection of clips from the Later archive. No stranger to Later and the Hootenanny, Cocker has performed on the show five times, the last in 2006 with music from his debut solo release. There are also performances by debutants Greentea Peng and Working Men's Club

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: The ship manages to rejoin the fleet, but at a heavy price - a Cylon computer virus has found its way into the Galactica's system. Colonel Tigh fears the Centurions are planning to murder the crew and seize control of the vessel. Meanwhile, Number Six gives Baltar some unnerving news

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: While Adama recovers from surgery after being shot, the crash survivors on Kobol learn of an anti-aircraft weapon deployed by the Cylons to take out the rescue ship. Tigh realises the magnitude of his role as stand-in Commander when the Quorum of Twelve challenges his authority

programme.title: The Awakening (2011)
programme.desc: In 1920s England, paranormal investigator Florence Cathcart travels around the country exposing supposed supernatural occurrences as hoaxes. She is summoned to a remote boarding school to examine reports of a haunting, but finds her rational disbelief in the occult challenged by the disturbing events that unfold there. Horror, starring Rebecca Hall, Dominic West and Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Coronavirus has hit the whole nation hard, but for the under-25s the impact has been particularly difficult. Reporter Kash Jones investigates the long-term consequences of the pandemic on young people, revealing exclusive research about the impact of Covid-19 on them and the scale of the challenges ahead. He meets young adults trying to deal with the impact on their education, job prospects and mental health

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Surgeons at Royal Papworth work to completely rebuild the chest wall of a patient who had to have a cancerous lung removed, and has lived with a hole in his back ever since. Consultant thoracic surgeon Aman Coonar and plastic surgeon Charles Malata must take a flap of muscle and skin from Kevin's back and painstakingly attach the blood vessels to fill the cavity and plug the hole. At Addenbrooke, a leading neurosurgeon must remove a potentially fatal tumour from the back of a patient's brain

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood show more clips from the natural world, including a toad that grows its babies on its back, and a two-headed snake

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie meets komodo dragons in Indonesia

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The gang gets in a spooky mood for Halloween

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia sets Vinny up on a series of strange dates. Wallace gets engaged to a cake

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Wallace thinks he has found the world's last dragon egg

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve's on a mission to find dinosaurs that have something a little special - fearsome killers, with incredible abilities. His quest sees him race in the dark like a Troodon, track prey from miles away taking tips from the Daspletosaurus, and finally gets his hands on a real life Caiman to show how their pressure pits are just like that of the Spinosaurus

programme.title: The Big Life Fix with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: A team of inventors try to help people in need, creating solutions to their problems. Forty-year-old Graham was a passionate snowboarder until an accident changed his life. Now severely disabled, can anyone help him realise his dream of standing upright on a board again? Others requiring help are Alexander, who could die if his skin is exposed to sunlight, and trainee hairdresser Kyle, whose hopes of becoming a professional stylist are hampered by his deformed hand

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present another round of battles in the arena. An untested robot built by a cardiologist and his two sons makes a surprising impact in the arena, while a builder from Northampton wheels out an impressive looking axe-wielding robot. Plus, judge Sethu Vijayakumar discusses his work with Nasa, designing robots to explore Mars

programme.title: World's Sneakiest Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham features a host of animals that use deceptive tactics to find food. Some trick their prey, like the manipulative mantis which looks like a flower and killer whales which set traps using sound. Others cheat each other - sea otters demand ransoms after kidnapping each other's pups - and some, like African bird the drongo and monkeys, fool other species. In parks, meanwhile, grey squirrels are at war in stealing raids and it seems these animal liars, cheats and hustlers really will stop at nothing to get a meal

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate British markets and the produce sold at them, making a rich walnut and fudge cake and preparing two seasonal soups. They also travel to Ludlow, Shropshire, to check out the stalls at a food festival and discuss the impact supermarkets have had on what people eat

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Kelly family from Aberdeen spend a trial week living in New Zealand, but the idea of leaving their loving family behind proves difficult

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple with a £650,000 budget to make the 500-mile move from a Scottish city to the English countryside around Wiltshire. Away from the house hunting, Jonnie joins a farmer, his dog and his flock for a husbandry masterclass with a unique breed of sheep native to the county

programme.title: The Band Wagon (1953)
programme.desc: A has-been Hollywood dancer and a pretentious producer form an uneasy alliance to stage a song-and-dance spectacular. However, the latter's bizarre ideas soon spiral out of control, while the former loathes the ballerina cast opposite him. Musical comedy, starring Fred Astaire, Jack Buchanan and Cyd Charisse. Songs include That's Entertainment and Dancing in the Dark

programme.title: Astaire and Rogers Sing the Great American Songbook
programme.desc: A compilation of hits performed by Fred Astaire and Ginger Rogers, all written by renowned American songwriters including George and Ira Gershwin, Irving Berlin, Jerome Kern and Dorothy Fields, Harold Arlen and Johnny Mercer

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents highlights from some of the historic venues up and down the country and goes behind the scenes of Bamburgh Castle in Northumberland

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael reflects on his fourth travel series, exploring the Sahara. This journey brought him into contact with nomads, refugees and artists who had sought out this vast desert, but also those displaced by war, the migrant crisis and the rise of militant Islam. The journey took Michael over the Atlas Mountains, to a Smara refugee camp, to the famed city of Timbuktu, and into the deep desert as part of a camel train

programme.title: Scotland from the Sky
programme.desc: Jamie Crawford takes to the skies using aerial images to discover how people in Scotland have lived off the land over the centuries, combining old aerial photographs with present day drone and helicopter footage to tell a range of stories, from the whisky of Islay to the fertile fields of the Lothians

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Ireland (Kick-off 8.10pm). John Inverdale presents coverage of the final match of the championship, which comes from Stade de France. Tonight's hosts looked in a strong position to win the Grand Slam after victories over England and Wales, but they then suffered a shock defeat at the hands of Scotland, though they are still in with a chance of securing the title. However, Ireland also go into this match in contention and are unbeaten in their last three Six Nations matches here. With analysis from Martin Johnson and Paul O'Connell, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Brian Moore and Jamie Heaslip

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig quizzes the panel on a few revolutionary ideas, with Alan Davies, Gyles Brandreth, Susan Calman and Jessica Fostekew going round the houses trying to come up with a decent answer

programme.title: Make Up (2019)
programme.desc: On a remote holiday park in Cornwall, reserved teenager Ruth is drawn into a mysterious obsession when she finds a smeared lipstick stain on her boyfriend Tom's mirror and a strand of red hair on his T-shirt. And when she starts to work with the confident and vivacious Jade, her scarlet nails and the hint of red in her hair amplifies Ruth's growing fixation. Drama, starring Molly Windsor, Joseph Quinn and Stefanie Martini

programme.title: Poltergeist (1982)
programme.desc: A family experiences strange events after invisible phantoms invade its home. At first the ghosts seem harmless, simply making things move by themselves. But before long, they spirit away the five-year-old daughter to their own plane of existence, forcing her parents to call on paranormal researchers to find their missing child. Horror, with JoBeth Williams, Craig T Nelson and Heather O'Rourke

programme.title: Our Little Sister (2015)
programme.desc: Three sisters who share a house together receive word that their estranged father has died. They travel to attend his funeral, only to discover he has a teenage daughter by another mother, who is now an orphan. They invite the girl to live with them, sparking a journey of self-discovery for all four women. Drama based on Akimi Yoshida's comic-book series, starring Haruka Ayase, Masami Nagasawa, Kaho and Suzu Hirose. In Japanese

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood's alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture continues with the letter M. Various BBC gardening presenters are mesmerised by magnolias, marvel at the monkey puzzle tree and meet a marigold enthusiast

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Lake Vyrnwy in Powys, exploring how industry and conservation work hand-in-hand to safeguard important habitats there. Ellie gets hands-on with woodland management in a bid to protect a rare day-flying moth, while Matt discovers the fascinating story of the Bank of the Black Ox. After a disappointing harvest, Adam Henson is hoping some new crops will make for an improved yield, while Charlotte Smith investigates concerns that the UK's environmental watchdogs aren't doing enough to protect wildlife and their habitats

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don harvests his pumpkins at Longmeadow, plants bulbs in a pot and has plenty of jobs for everyone to be getting on with over the coming weeks. Frances Tophill travels to Winkworth Arboretum in Surrey to celebrate the season and to explain the science behind the golden and red tones of autumn leaves. The team take inspiration from RHS Harlow Carr and take a look at their winter borders and the plants which will add interest and drama to gardens over the coming months

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond is in the Cairngorms hoping to see the annual red deer rut. Euan McIlwraith celebrates the colour red in the landscape and Arlene Stuart is picking acorns

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Following gardeners in Orkney, Shetland and the Black Isle as they show how they have created stunning sites right on the very edges of Scotland in difficult conditions

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate British dairy products, preparing a classic cheese and onion turnover, a trinity cream and a number of traditional milk puddings

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg and Chris are in Bromley with the Winbourne family, who desperately want to change their ways. Busy mum Clare and daughters Rachel and Sadie all rely on dad James to cook the family meals, but not everyone is satisfied with what's being rustled up. With the same meals on rotation and a lack of fruit and veg being consumed, the whole family are bored when it comes to mealtimes, and often resort to eating out

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A profile of actor Kenneth More, featuring archive interviews and clips capturing the milestones and highlights in his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Reach for the Sky (1956)
programme.desc: Biopic of Second World War hero Douglas Bader. A plane crash in 1931 cost the aspiring pilot both his legs, but after learning to walk on artificial limbs, he went on to become one of the RAF's most decorated heroes. However, more misfortune was in store when he was shot down over occupied France and taken prisoner. Starring Kenneth More, Muriel Pavlow and Alexander Knox

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Gavin & Stacey co-creator and star Ruth Jones traces her family tree, learning that her paternal grandfather was a leading figure in the Medical Aid Societies of South Wales - which provided a model for the National Health Service in 1948. On her maternal side, she discovers that her great-great-great-grandfather was a mariner who rose to the rank of captain in the domestic coastal trade

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Cherry Healey shed light on how some of Britain's biggest factories make the nation's favourite products, beginning with popular breakfast cereals. Gregg starts by receiving a boat-load of corn fresh from Argentina, and witnesses as it is cooked, milled and flavoured to become a top-selling cereal brand. Cherry learns about the immunity-boosting powers of vitamin D, which is added to many of the UK's most popular cereals, and historian Ruth Gordon samples a Victorian breakfast of lobster and pig's head, as part of a daily meal that included 4,500 calories

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Jenny Campbell and Tej Lalvani assess the profit-making potential of more business ideas, including a keen fisherman with a plan to market his own range of bait, a pasta home delivery service, and a brother and sister with a scheme to make money out of rubbish. Evan Davis presents

programme.title: A Life on Screen
programme.desc: The actor and writer looks back on his career in an in-depth interview recorded after he received a special award at the 2013 Baftas. The programme looks back at his varied television career, from his earliest collaborations with writing partner Terry Jones, his innovative breakthrough as one of the stars of Monty Python's Flying Circus, to his later dramatic roles and award-winning series of travelogues. Featuring contributions from those who have worked with Palin, including John Cleese, David Jason and Armando Iannucci

programme.title: Totally Under Control (2020)
programme.desc: An in-depth look at how the United States government handled the response to the Covid-19 outbreak during the early months of the pandemic. The film examines the decision-making that led the US to suffer the world's worst casualty rate during the crisis, with over 200,000 deaths. With contributions from public health officials

programme.title: Beatriz at Dinner (2017)
programme.desc: A holistic medicine practitioner visits a wealthy client at home, only to find herself stranded when her car breaks down. The client invites her to attend a dinner party she is throwing that night, where she is drawn into a battle of wills with her privileged fellow guests. Comedy drama, starring Salma Hayek and John Lithgow

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Monica develops new abilities with Micah's help, while Alejandro and his sister continue their trek to the US. Matt puts Molly's life in jeopardy when he asks her to locate the man in her dreams, and Claire becomes suspicious of her father. Drama, starring Dana Davis, Greg Grunberg and Adair Tishler

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Matt visits his father Maury and takes along Nathan as they search for answers regarding Molly's nightmare and the death threats received by Angela and Kaito. Meanwhile, a new hero appears and attempts to find Peter. Kristen Bell, Alan Blumenfeld and Adrian Pasdar star

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from the audience. Presented by Fiona Bruce

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Cultural review show offering a mixture of interviews, news features and performances from the world of the arts

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Host Sara Cox is joined by contributors Rebecca Front, Sophie Ward, Ade Adepitan and Bill Bailey to discuss this week's book group picks - Fifty-Fifty by Steve Cavanagh and Sophie Ward's Love and Other Thought Experiments

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Sound of Movie Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: Once the Second World War ended, the American musical began to thrive with a colour and exuberance even more pronounced than before, entering a second Golden Age. Central to its success, the composer and musician argues, was the emergence of Gene Kelly. Talking with Kelly's widow, Patricia Ward Kelly, Brand looks at how this star brought the musical film into the streets of America, most famously with Singin' in the Rain, believed to be one of the greatest films ever made of any genre. Brand also takes a look at significant Indian movies of this period, as well as some made in China

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first aired on February 15, with performances by artists including Sinead O'Connor, The Wedding Present and Black Box

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first aired on February 22, with performances by artists including Tina Turner, Beats International and Adam Ant

programme.title: Pop Charts Britannia: 60 Years of the Top 10
programme.desc: The evolution of feelings and attitudes toward the British singles chart. Documenting changes from the 1952 NME countdown via Pick and Top of the Pops through to the Radio 1 chart show of 2012. With contributions from chart show presenters David Jacobs and Reggie Yates, music fans Grace Dent and Pete Paphides and veteran musicians Jon Webster and Rob Dickins

programme.title: A Musical History
programme.desc: A multi-faceted genius whose music has permeated popular culture, Stevie Wonder is not short of celebrity fans. Here, some well-known fans celebrate the man and his music by selecting some of his best-loved songs. Contributors include actor Martin Freeman, singers Alexander O'Neal, James Morrison, Beverley Knight and Corinne Bailey Rae, New Order's Gillian Gilbert and Stephen Morris, DJs Ana Matronic, Trevor Nelson and Norman Jay, Heaven's 17's Glenn Gregory and Martyn Ware, journalist Sian Pattenden and presenter Emma Dabiri

programme.title: Sings Stevie Wonder
programme.desc: A trawl through the BBC archives, unearthing 50 years of covers of Wonder's classic songbook, featuring artists including Cilla Black, Jimmy Helms, Dionne Warwick, the Osmonds, India Arie and James Morrison. Among the clips is a storming performance of Ed Sheeran with Jools Holland and his Rhythm & Blues Orchestra taking on Master Blaster (Jammin')

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: The singer-songwriter performs an intimate concert at the Maida Vale Studios in London. Featuring new songs from Still on My Mind, along with hits from No Angel and Life for Rent

programme.title: Pop Charts Britannia: 60 Years of the Top 10
programme.desc: The evolution of feelings and attitudes toward the British singles chart. Documenting changes from the 1952 NME countdown via Pick and Top of the Pops through to the Radio 1 chart show of 2012. With contributions from chart show presenters David Jacobs and Reggie Yates, music fans Grace Dent and Pete Paphides and veteran musicians Jon Webster and Rob Dickins

programme.title: The Sound of Movie Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: Once the Second World War ended, the American musical began to thrive with a colour and exuberance even more pronounced than before, entering a second Golden Age. Central to its success, the composer and musician argues, was the emergence of Gene Kelly. Talking with Kelly's widow, Patricia Ward Kelly, Brand looks at how this star brought the musical film into the streets of America, most famously with Singin' in the Rain, believed to be one of the greatest films ever made of any genre. Brand also takes a look at significant Indian movies of this period, as well as some made in China

programme.title: Inside the Batcave
programme.desc: Lucy Cooke presents a documentary taking a journey into the secret world of one of the most endangered and least understood animals on Earth - bats. With cutting-edge night-vision cameras and ultrasonic detectors, this programme follows a greater horseshoe bat roost for four months, capturing the hidden life of the colony as never before, witnessing the birth of a new generation of pups and tracking their progress towards their perilous maiden flights outside the roost

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The dangerous Sulu Sea fails to deter Michael Palin as he clambers aboard a rusty old ferry bound for deepest Borneo. Here he meets orang-utans and a friendly rainforest tribe once known for headhunting. Finally, the intrepid actor crosses the equator en route to Java, where he visits the Buddhist temple of Borobudur. He also climbs an active volcano - but it's not long before Mother Nature ruins his plans to travel to Australia in style

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: The Minna case is reopened after Rolf and Neel find serious errors in the Danish DNA register, and Rolf and his former boss Astrid Oxlev assist the French police - with Claire Bobain (Charlotte Rampling) the lead investigator. In Danish, English, Polish and French

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: With clues pointing to Poland, the team heads there to find a crucial witness in possession of a video, but Claire is concerned that Rolf seems more focused on his daughter's case than with the murder they are currently investigating. In Danish, English, Polish and French

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Steve Wright introduces an edition of the music show featuring the spookiest, scariest and most frightening clips from the pop archives involving witches, monsters, ghosts, the Addams Family and Dracula. Including performances by the Automatic, the Crazy World of Arthur Brown, Ray Parker Jr, Bobby 'Boris' Pickett and Michael Jackson

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Burglars become entangled in the hunt for the killer when they unwittingly uncover two new victims. Henrik immediately realises the state of the bodies reflects another Freddie Holst art work - Cancel Christmas. The murdered pair were foster carers, and while Saga and Henrik await the arrival of a list of their charges, John's new girlfriend Tina provides an intriguing new lead. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Emil Larsson is found injured and dazed, prompting police to reconsider his status as a suspect. Following leads he gives them from his hospital bed, Saga, Henrik and John locate the property in which he was held captive, and discover it is owned by another of the Johansson's foster children. Meanwhile, the internal affairs investigation into the death of Saga's mother continues, placing the detective under further pressure. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Anthea Turner hosts the edition first aired on February 15, with performances by artists including Sinead O'Connor, The Wedding Present and Black Box

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first aired on February 22, with performances by artists including Tina Turner, Beats International and Adam Ant

programme.title: Inside the Batcave
programme.desc: Lucy Cooke presents a documentary taking a journey into the secret world of one of the most endangered and least understood animals on Earth - bats. With cutting-edge night-vision cameras and ultrasonic detectors, this programme follows a greater horseshoe bat roost for four months, capturing the hidden life of the colony as never before, witnessing the birth of a new generation of pups and tracking their progress towards their perilous maiden flights outside the roost

programme.title: Mud, Sweat and Tractors: The Story of Agriculture
programme.desc: The documentary examining British food production during the 20th century looks at how the Hereford and Aberdeen Angus breeds of cattle have evolved over the years, since the nation earned the reputation of 'the Stockyard of the World' before the Second World War

programme.title: Climbing Blind
programme.desc: Documentary about Jesse Dufton and his attempt to take on the challenge of becoming the first blind person to lead a climb of the Old Man of Hoy in Orkney, Scotland. At four years of age, Jesse was diagnosed with retinitis pigmentosa, a rare genetic disease that breaks down the retina's cells. When he was 20, Jesse could no longer read. By the time he was 30, he could only detect light, with around a one to two per cent field of view. As a lifelong climber, what Jesse has achieved flies in the face of adversity, training for world cup events and leading traditional rock climbs with his sight guide and fiancee Molly. Now, his attempt on the Old Man of Hoy is testimony to his ambition to take on new and greater challenges, despite his devastating condition

programme.title: Final Ascent: The Legend of Hamish MacInnes
programme.desc: Documentary about the famous mountaineer, inventor of the all metal ice axe and author of the International Mountain Rescue Handbook. Suffering from delirium in his eighties, he lost his memories, but was able to recover them using pictures taken over the previous 60 years and copies of the books he had written

programme.title: Happy End (2017)
programme.desc: Following an industrial accident at one of their building sites, the Laurent family are joined by 12-year-old Eve as her mother lies close to death in hospital. Writer/director Michael Haneke's drama, starring Mathieu Kassovitz and Toby Jones

programme.title: Dracula by Northern Ballet
programme.desc: Bram Stoker's gothic romance is brought to life on stage in this ballet, performed by the dancers of Northern Ballet to music by Schnittke, Rachmaninov, Pärt and Daugherty. Choreographed by David Nixon OBE and recorded at Leeds Playhouse on Halloween 2019

By the time he was 30, he could only detect light, with around a one to two per cent field of view. As a lifelong climber, what Jesse has achieved flies in the face of adversity, training for world cup events and leading traditional rock climbs with his sight guide and fiancee Molly. programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends go on a bizarre quest to find the most unusual, strange and amazing places to visit in the UK. In this episode, the team head to Stratford-upon-Avon for a Shakespearian word battle, eat their way around Orkney, take a zip line over Cornwall's Eden Project, visit a traditional east London pie, mash and jellied eels cafe with French food critic Rene Mangetout, and pay tribute to Bram Stoker on a gothic tour of Whitby, while Max and Harvey prank Andy Fowler from RoadTrip and Love Island's Samira Mighty with Jonas Brothers song titles. Ed is joined on this amazing and offbeat road trip by Annabelle Davis, Sam and Mark, Naomi Wilkinson, Inel Tomlinson, Kailem, Eman Kellam, Max and Harvey and The Gaming Beaver - bet you didn’t know this stuff was in the UK, but it turns up All Over the Place!

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: The brothers unveil their biggest and best enclosure yet, a huge new habitat for the Amur tiger sisters Aleena and Siberia. The boys test out the tigers new 80,000-gallon swimming pool and get hands-on as they prepare to move the world’s biggest cats. Meanwhile, the other animals at the park are treated to a bubble show, Tyler has to clean out the tapir toilet and the boys recall their travels to Kenya to see the world‘s last two remaining northern white rhinos.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: It’s the zoo’s grand reopening and the boys are preparing to open the gates to the public for the first time. The night before, they were rudely awoken, and Moto the lion took the opportunity to investigate what the animals get up to during the dead of night. Cam draws the short straw and has to clean out the camel enclosure, while Billy the ocelot is given a pungent treat to play with and the boys recall the extraordinary story of how the zoo was started. Meanwhile, the brothers catch up on when they met with rangers on the front line training to intercept and arrest wildlife poachers.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: Temperatures rise as the zoo is hit by a heatwave. Many of the animals are well adapted to the sun, but those from cooler climates need extra attention. The boys devise a series of ingenious tricks to keep each animal cool, including ice baths, mist showers and giant ice lollies. It’s fish flavour for the penguins, lettuce lollies for Marion the tortoise and a huge blood lolly for the big cats. Meanwhile, the camels get their yearly comb-down, Tyler has to muck out the stinky Penguin pool and the boys play with an orphaned elephant in Uganda.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: The boys welcome some new arrivals to the zoo. Mimo the Pygmy Marmoset emerges from its nest box for the first time and two penguin chicks hatch. Meanwhile, it’s Tilly the red panda’s birthday, and to celebrate, the brothers decide to hold a cake-off. It’s a tradition at the zoo that when a new animal in born, all the animals get presents. Finally, we catch up with the boys in Rwanda as they meet wildlife heroes fighting to save the planet’s largest primates - the mountain gorilla.

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: It’s health check day and the boys give each of the animals a thorough MOT. Tamara the tapir gets a pedicure, Marion the tortoise gets a shell rub and the keepers discover a worrying lump on Luna the reindeer’s leg. It's Aaron’s turn to clean out the giant anaconda enclosure, and the boys venture to Uganda to ride with the country’s first animal ambulance.

programme.desc: JoJo tries to grow a daffodil, but is disheartened to discover that it will take a long time to bloom. Cynthia shows JoJo how to take care of the plant, and Gran Gran shows JoJo a way to measure how much it is growing.

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Katie worries about Grannie Island, who having hurt her back and unable to get out of bed, does not want to trouble anyone. The youngster decides to ask Granma Mainland for help

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Flamingo and John are invited to Sarah and Duck's house, but a big obstacle lies in store for John

programme.title: Tee and Mo

programme.title: Clangers

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Eee gets separated from the Tombliboos Unn and Ooo, and needs Upsy Daisy's megaphone to find them

Taking part this time around are horse racing legend AP McCoy, World Cup-winning cricketer Charlotte Edwards, former England goalkeeper David James and Olympic cycling champion Joanna Rowsell Shand

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian and writer David Baddiel, former MP and Strictly Come Dancing 2016 star Ed Balls, stand-up and actress Jo Brand and ex-Pussycat Doll and Celebrity MasterChef 2015 winner Kimberly Wyatt join host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki proposes a poker game with Vinny and Kheerat at Ruby's club and suggests they invite Kush, but he is suspicious of her motive and declines. However, when he later turns up he finds them ready to play - and cannot resist joining in. Meanwhile, Shirley offers Gray a job in the Mitchell family business

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Channel 4 presenter Steph McGovern hosts the satirical quiz, inviting comedian Miles Jupp and Baroness Sayeeda Warsi to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Rhod trains to become a fighter pilot with the RAF, which is quite possibly his toughest and most frightening work experience yet, as the closest he has got to a jet up until now is watching Top Gun. His fears are realised when it comes to his final challenge - co-piloting on an exercise

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham is joined by Irish actor and singer Jessie Buckley, starring in Charlie Kaufman's Netflix drama I'm Thinking of Ending Things. Also appearing are comedian Bill Bailey - one of this year's Strictly contestants - plus fellow comic and children's author David Walliams. Singer Dermot Kennedy performs his single Giants, and the host offers a wry look at some trending news stories

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce is challenged to live with a capsule wardrobe - selecting just 14 items from her vast collection of clothes and wearing nothing else for two weeks. She discovers what becomes of discarded clothes with a visit to a landfill site and investigates the work of online influencers in keeping the public hooked on buying the latest new look

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Edmund and Nora prepare to launch their app, and Wally's dinner date goes terribly awry

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: After Scrubr gets acquired by a company overseas, Nora moves to China. Back in Queens, Grandma befriends an injured pigeon

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at the interview stage and a helpful guide on how to survive the process, which sees the last few candidates go toe-to-toe with Alan Sugar's trusted business associates - Claude Littner, Linda Plant and Mike Soutar - as their CVs and business plans are heavily scrutinised

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Bryn Williams, and special guest Nigella Lawson. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook heads to Snowdonia to hear the real-life Romeo and Juliet story of Vincenzo Algieri and Betty Evans - two star-crossed lovers who, despite being on opposite sides of the Second World War, found love together against all the odds. From different cultures and speaking different languages, Vincenzo and Betty bonded over food - traditional Italian dishes the family still treasures to this day. Grandson Huw has some clues of the tale the lies within his family history, but hopes Michela's specialist knowledge can help fill in some of the blanks

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Liverpool v West Ham United and Manchester United v Arsenal

programme.title: The Wedding Dress Shop
programme.desc: A bride undergoing breast cancer treatment goes for a fitting, but is shocked to discover Samm intends to take a pair of scissors to the dress while she is wearing it. A woman with fragile confidence who never normally wears dresses gives her wedding gown the seal of approval - with some support from her mother and mother-in-law

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Wales v Scotland (Kick-off 2.15pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from both teams' fifth and final match of the championship, held at Parc y Scarlets. The Welsh have failed to repeat their heroics of last year when they won the Grand Slam, losing three of their four contests. Meanwhile, Scotland started their campaign with two defeats, but then won in Italy before ending France's hope of a Grand Slam with a shock 28-17 triumph at Murrayfield. With analysis from Jeremy Guscott and Andy Nicol, and commentary by Andrew Cotter, Jonathan Davies and Chris Paterson

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Doctor and television presenter Ranj Singh, former TOWIE cast member Jessica Wright, author and former politician Alan Johnson and comedian and writer Katy Brand brave the famous black chair answering questions on RuPaul's Drag Race, the character Monica Geller from Friends, pop group the Beatles and the movie Dirty Dancing. John Humphrys presents

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The girl group search for the next global singing sensation, with the winning band joining them on their next UK tour. Tonight, the five remaining bands battle it out in tonight's semi-final

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the 12 couples return to the Strictly Ballroom for an epic night of dance. The stakes could not be higher because not only do the Strictly stars have to impress the judges Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse, but they also have to impress the viewers at home, who get the chance to vote for their favourites for the very first time this series

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Ireland. John Inverdale presents second-half coverage of the final match of the championship, which comes from Stade de France. Tonight's hosts looked in a strong position to win the Grand Slam after victories over England and Wales, but they then suffered a shock defeat at the hands of Scotland, though they are still in with a chance of securing the title. However, Ireland also go into this match in contention and are unbeaten in their last three Six Nations matches here. With analysis from Martin Johnson and Paul O'Connell, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Brian Moore and Jamie Heaslip

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Liverpool v West Ham United at Anfield, Burnley v Chelsea at Turf Moor and Sheffield United v Manchester City at Bramall Lane. The Reds were made to work hard for a 3-2 home win over the Hammers last season, while Chelsea and City will both have been hoping to emulate their away victories over their opponents in the previous campaign

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Nat Coombs is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Carolina Panthers v Atlanta Falcons and look ahead to the rest of the week eight fixtures

programme.title: The Ring (2002)
programme.desc: A journalist investigates several teenage deaths, which are linked to a cursed videotape and the rumour that anyone who watches it will die a week later. Her growing obsession with the tape endangers herself and her family - and to avoid the predicted fate, she has just seven days to find out where the recording came from. Gore Verbinski's remake of the Japanese supernatural horror, starring Naomi Watts, Martin Henderson and Brian Cox

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow projects to make family homes out of a three-bed semi in Stoke-on-Trent, a split-level maisonette in Brixton, and a detached property in Surrey

programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits Dundee, home of the Beano and the Dandy, to see how its comics tradition inspired a new version of the Bible. With music by Barbara Dickson and Cliff Richard

programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Live Women's FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Everton v Manchester City (Kick-off 2.30pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from the final at Wembley Stadium. Everton are looking to lift the trophy for the first time since the 2009/10 season and have impressed since the competition resumed with victories over Chelsea and Everton. The Citizens have overcome Leicester City and Arsenal since the restart and will now have high hopes of winning the trophy for the third successive season

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway goes on another walk around Wales in his efforts to get people to enjoy the great outdoors on their doorstep

programme.title: Coal House
programme.desc: The families preparations for the final concert are added to their already exhausting workload and consider their experiences so far. Nicola Reynolds narrates

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the sixth annual event, the team heads out across the UK to lead rambles and meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. Matt Baker is in the Peak District to take on challenge with 16-year-old Harvey, who was supported by bereavement counselling after his brother was killed, while Steve Brown is inspired to attempt scaling Wales' Pen y Fan mountain in his wheelchair after meeting 5-year-old Emerson, a sport-lover with a spinal-cord injury

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from the first two live weekends. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel-Horwood and Motsi Mabuse decide who will be the first pair to leave the contest. With music by Sam Smith

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness introduces Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff to his latest creation - an all-electric, all-terrain ice-cream van for the 21st century. Elsewhere, the team tests the battery-powered Honda E, Vauxhall Corsa-e and Mini Electric with a race around a deserted Alton Towers

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: Confronted about his affair, pressure mounts on the home front for Peter, while the party chairman asks him about his ambitions. Is he after the top job? Rochelle decides to pick up where Charmian left off

programme.title: Dark Land: Hunting the Killers
programme.desc: Documentary reinvestigating some of the country's most shocking unsolved murders, beginning with the case of 23-year-old Port Talbot woman Maureen Mulcahy, who was found strangled to death in February 1976. After a major breakthrough, the team discovers clues that suggest she may been the victim of a serial killer, and a new witness comes forward with new testimony

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With double Olympic taekwondo champion Jade Jones, England rugby union winger Ugo Monye, gold medal-winning hockey star Sam Quek and former Manchester United defender Gary Pallister. Sue Barker asks the questions, with team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell
