programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the sixth annual event, the team heads out across the UK to lead rambles and meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. Matt Baker is in the Peak District to take on challenge with 16-year-old Harvey, who was supported by bereavement counselling after his brother was killed, while Steve Brown is inspired to attempt scaling Wales' Pen y Fan mountain in his wheelchair after meeting 5-year-old Emerson, a sport-lover with a spinal-cord injury

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from the first two live weekends. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel-Horwood and Motsi Mabuse decide who will be the first pair to leave the contest. With music by Sam Smith

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness introduces Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff to his latest creation - an all-electric, all-terrain ice-cream van for the 21st century. Elsewhere, the team tests the battery-powered Honda E, Vauxhall Corsa-e and Mini Electric with a race around a deserted Alton Towers

programme.title: Roadkill
programme.desc: Confronted about his affair, pressure mounts on the home front for Peter, while the party chairman asks him about his ambitions. Is he after the top job? Rochelle decides to pick up where Charmian left off

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, introducing highlights of Manchester United v Arsenal at Old Trafford and Tottenham Hotspur v Brighton & Hove Albion at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium. Plus, action from Aston Villa v Southampton at Villa Park and Newcastle United v Everton at St James' Park

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Steven Thompson presents action from the Scottish Cup semi-final between Aberdeen and Celtic at Hampden Park. Plus, Kilmarnock v Rangers at Rugby Park in the Premiership

programme.title: Women's Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the final round of fixtures in the annual international rugby union tournament, featuring France v Ireland, Wales v Scotland and Italy v England

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Crewmates Adam and Dan treat a patient who has fallen down a flight of stairs in a casino, while Rachel and Stuart respond to a call when a child falls from a bunk bed. As the shift continues, a distressing call from a patient threatening to jump into the river takes priority, and the call handler must try to keep the patient talking until help can arrive

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the development progress of properties in Pimlico, London, Ilkeston, Derbyshire and Chesterton, near Newcastle-under-Lyme

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: In Birmingham, the investigators crack down on the callous travel agent ripping off British holidaymakers, taking their money but never booking their flights. In light of the story of one limo driving a school party of 11-year-olds bursting into flames, there's also some spot-checking on luxury limos being driven in London

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A couple whose car was hit by a truck on the M1 while on a journey to celebrate their wedding anniversary. Plus, a wing suit free-fall enthusiast who hit trouble when his main parachute became tangled up, 30 seconds from the ground. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In a rock and pop special, Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to the iconic BBC Maida Vale music studios, and gets the lowdown on an incredible collection of music memorabilia

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score a lint roller that specialises in pet hair, a storage solution for tin cans, and a plastic stick that can be shaped to help with various DIY tasks

programme.title: Escape to the Perfect Town
programme.desc: Sonali Shah makes a beeline for the desirable town of Sevenoaks in Kent, to help buyers who are selling up their London flat and sinking their £900,000 budget into a new life

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore searches for tired and unwanted items at Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey she can transform from skip-bound to stylish. With three items saved, Sarah turns to upholsterer Sharon O'Connor to overhaul an old chair frame, while upcycler Jacqui Joseph attempts to transform a dated desk. Sarah's challenge is to update a set of four chairs. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers and valuation expert Simon Bower welcome four more people with interesting items they'd like to sell. Nigel then invites the dealers to bid against one another to secure a vintage fairground game, a 19th-century Chinese inkwell, a 1950s toy pedal car and a Victorian watchman's clock

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances. Presented by Alex Jones

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker hosts the light-hearted quiz, with Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell heading teams of celebrities in a test of their sporting knowledge. Taking part this time around are horse racing legend AP McCoy, World Cup-winning cricketer Charlotte Edwards, former England goalkeeper David James and Olympic cycling champion Joanna Rowsell Shand

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Heavy snow disrupts plans for the wedding as travel to Button House becomes near impossible, so Alison and Mike have to rally round to make sure Sam and Clare's big day goes ahead. The Captain is in his element with the emergency preparations to the house, but Pat is distracted by an unexpected reminder of his past, while Lady Button is aghast at what Alison is allowing to take place at her former home

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki proposes a poker game with Vinny and Kheerat at Ruby's club and suggests they invite Kush, but he is suspicious of her motive and declines. However, when he later turns up he finds them ready to play - and cannot resist joining in. Meanwhile, Shirley offers Gray a job in the Mitchell family business

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Channel 4 presenter Steph McGovern hosts the satirical quiz, inviting comedian Miles Jupp and Baroness Sayeeda Warsi to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Royle Family
programme.desc: With Dave and Denise's wedding fast approaching, the arrival of a huge phone bill is the last thing Jim needs. Another chance to see the first-ever episode of the award-winning comedy, starring Caroline Aherne, Craig Cash, Ricky Tomlinson and Sue Johnston

programme.title: Previously with Shane Todd
programme.desc: The comedian casts a millennial's eye over the rich pickings of the BBC archive. He continues by exploring the subject of 'beauty', with clips of bodybuilders, a Bunny Girl and finding out how much it would take for the people of Belfast to pose nude

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham is joined by Irish actor and singer Jessie Buckley, starring in Charlie Kaufman's Netflix drama I'm Thinking of Ending Things. Also appearing are comedian Bill Bailey - one of this year's Strictly contestants - plus fellow comic and children's author David Walliams. Singer Dermot Kennedy performs his single Giants, and the host offers a wry look at some trending news stories

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Edmund and Nora prepare to launch their app, and Wally's dinner date goes terribly awry

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: After Scrubr gets acquired by a company overseas, Nora moves to China. Back in Queens, Grandma befriends an injured pigeon

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at the interview stage and a helpful guide on how to survive the process, which sees the last few candidates go toe-to-toe with Alan Sugar's trusted business associates - Claude Littner, Linda Plant and Mike Soutar - as their CVs and business plans are heavily scrutinised

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Bryn Williams, and special guest Nigella Lawson. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook heads to Snowdonia to hear the real-life Romeo and Juliet story of Vincenzo Algieri and Betty Evans - two star-crossed lovers who, despite being on opposite sides of the Second World War, found love together against all the odds. From different cultures and speaking different languages, Vincenzo and Betty bonded over food - traditional Italian dishes the family still treasures to this day. Grandson Huw has some clues of the tale the lies within his family history, but hopes Michela's specialist knowledge can help fill in some of the blanks

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Liverpool v West Ham United and Manchester United v Arsenal

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross oversees oversees proceedings at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells as two teams scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell on for profit. Experts Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant attempt to guide the sides to success at auction in Cardiff

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Wales v Scotland (Kick-off 2.15pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from both teams' fifth and final match of the championship, held at Parc y Scarlets. The Welsh have failed to repeat their heroics of last year when they won the Grand Slam, losing three of their four contests. Meanwhile, Scotland started their campaign with two defeats, but then won in Italy before ending France's hope of a Grand Slam with a shock 28-17 triumph at Murrayfield. With analysis from Jeremy Guscott and Andy Nicol, and commentary by Andrew Cotter, Jonathan Davies and Chris Paterson

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: Doctor and television presenter Ranj Singh, former TOWIE cast member Jessica Wright, author and former politician Alan Johnson and comedian and writer Katy Brand brave the famous black chair answering questions on RuPaul's Drag Race, the character Monica Geller from Friends, pop group the Beatles and the movie Dirty Dancing. John Humphrys presents

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The girl group search for the next global singing sensation, with the winning band joining them on their next UK tour. Tonight, the five remaining bands battle it out in tonight's semi-final

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the 12 couples return to the Strictly Ballroom for an epic night of dance. The stakes could not be higher because not only do the Strictly stars have to impress the judges Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse, but they also have to impress the viewers at home, who get the chance to vote for their favourites for the very first time this series

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Ireland. John Inverdale presents second-half coverage of the final match of the championship, which comes from Stade de France. Tonight's hosts looked in a strong position to win the Grand Slam after victories over England and Wales, but they then suffered a shock defeat at the hands of Scotland, though they are still in with a chance of securing the title. However, Ireland also go into this match in contention and are unbeaten in their last three Six Nations matches here. With analysis from Martin Johnson and Paul O'Connell, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Brian Moore and Jamie Heaslip

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Liverpool v West Ham United at Anfield, Burnley v Chelsea at Turf Moor and Sheffield United v Manchester City at Bramall Lane. The Reds were made to work hard for a 3-2 home win over the Hammers last season, while Chelsea and City will both have been hoping to emulate their away victories over their opponents in the previous campaign

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Nat Coombs is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Carolina Panthers v Atlanta Falcons and look ahead to the rest of the week eight fixtures

programme.title: The Ring (2002)
programme.desc: A journalist investigates several teenage deaths, which are linked to a cursed videotape and the rumour that anyone who watches it will die a week later. Her growing obsession with the tape endangers herself and her family - and to avoid the predicted fate, she has just seven days to find out where the recording came from. Gore Verbinski's remake of the Japanese supernatural horror, starring Naomi Watts, Martin Henderson and Brian Cox

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Liverpool v West Ham United at Anfield, Burnley v Chelsea at Turf Moor and Sheffield United v Manchester City at Bramall Lane. The Reds were made to work hard for a 3-2 home win over the Hammers last season, while Chelsea and City will both have been hoping to emulate their away victories over their opponents in the previous campaign

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Barra on the Bann
programme.desc: The final part of Barra Best's journey starts at the point where the Lower Bann begins at the northern side of Lough Neagh. He receives a rowing lesson from Coleraine's Olympic medal-winning brothers Peter and Richard Chambers, goes in search of the elusive otter, and reflects on the beauty, scale and surprise of Northern Ireland's longest river as his trip ends

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Kiwi-born Felicity stayed in the UK to raise her daughter, but now wants to relocate to Australia. However, daughter Lucy says she doesn't want to go, so the pair sample a week 'Down Under'. At the end of the trial, Lucy has to vote on where her future lies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow projects to make family homes out of a three-bed semi in Stoke-on-Trent, a split-level maisonette in Brixton, and a detached property in Surrey

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits Dundee, home of the Beano and the Dandy, to see how its comics tradition inspired a new version of the Bible. With music by Barbara Dickson and Cliff Richard

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Live Women's FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Everton v Manchester City (Kick-off 2.30pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from the final at Wembley Stadium. Everton are looking to lift the trophy for the first time since the 2009/10 season and have impressed since the competition resumed with victories over Chelsea and Everton. The Citizens have overcome Leicester City and Arsenal since the restart and will now have high hopes of winning the trophy for the third successive season

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design a garden for a family of seven in Stamford, Lincolnshire, who have just moved from rural Rutland and miss the countryside. With five noisy children, the parents want a peaceful garden that they can call their own, but the awkward plot gives the presenters a huge challenge, despite a budget of £4,500

programme.title: Air an Smùid (Steaming)
programme.desc: A biting, cold winter delays exterior work on the puffer so Duncan keeps himself occupied by taking on the restoration of a vintage Ford truck. When work resumes on the VIC 27, the team sets to power-washing and sandblasting the hull. This removes decades worth of rust and scale - and leaves gaping great holes. The re-plating of the heavily corroded metal looks likely to be time consuming and costly

programme.title: Vets: Gach Creutair Beo
programme.desc: At Glasgow Vet School, Professor Donald Yool is in charge of a particularly risky operation on a Bichon Frise dog, to remove a tumour that is attached to her main blood vessel

programme.title: Luther 500: An t-Stri Spioradail (The Battle for Souls)
programme.desc: In a journey that takes him from Rome, Germany and Geneva to the Outer Hebrides and across the island of Ireland, Alex O'Henley uncovers why the Gaelic-speaking areas of Scotland became, to a large extent, Protestant while most of Ireland remained Catholic. He shows how Martin Luther's legacy still looms large over the Gaelic world

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which members of a baseball team from Newport answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge. They then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Ken, Zoe, Keith and Amber work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tracy gets a call from Vanessa, Cain is shocked, and Matty worries for Moira

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Scott offers Emma a small fortune towards Oliver's fund for her silence, Geoff attempts to throw Alya off his trail with an OAP decoy, and Abi wonders if she and Kevin will ever be rid of Debbie

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A mother of two is found murdered in her back garden. Vera and Aiden investigate the case, and delve into the disturbing secrets and ongoing conflicts that lurk beneath the surface of the victim's seemingly peaceful suburban community. The case takes a more complicated turn when the dead woman's estranged family make an unexpected reappearance, and while several of her neighbours may have had cause to carry out a crime of passion, her relatives may have had compelling motives of their own to kill. Guest starring Rita Tushingham

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Grand Slam Years
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke presents a look back at England's victorious Six Nations campaign, when they bounced back from a dismal 2015 World Cup tow in all their matches in the championship

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: Jonathan is joined in the studio by guests comedian Russell Howard, classical singer Katherine Jenkins and Line of Duty star Daniel Mays. Rapper Dizzee Rascal will be chatting about his career and performing live in the studio

programme.title: Sorry, I Didn't Know
programme.desc: Shaun Wallace, Paul Chowdhry, Toby Williams and Sophie Duker join team leaders Chizzy Akudolu and Judi Love on the panel show, and take viewers on an educational and tongue-in-cheek tour of Black British history. Hosted by Jimmy Akingbola

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha joins forces with Mandy Yin, the guru of Malaysian cuisine, to create a family feast from Malaysia. The menu comprises Kaya French toast for breakfast, king prawn laksa for lunch, Nadia's chicken curry for dinner and Mandy's ble pandan pancakes for dessert

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Busy observing the sky in search of UFOs, Magoo accidentally finds himself wandering over to Fizz's house.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is thinks is out trick-or-treating for Halloween. But he unwittingly robs a bank instead of Weasel.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz has invented a machine which stops toast from landing butter-side down. Magoo sees some floating toast and believes a ghost is haunting their house.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Gordon and Alan travel to one of Jupiter's moons to rescue a party of astronauts who have become stranded under the ice

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: A trip to the cinema to see a spooky film scares Bean half to death

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Animated series serving as a prequel to the popular film series. Mavis and her friends learn the true meaning of Halloween.

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows a young boy and his two friends as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: Our mystery solving gang agrees to help Ricky Gervais with a haunted antique Egyptian statue of a cat.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by guest Emily Atack, and prepares oriental crab, Spanish meatballs and toffee apples. Chef Dipna Anand will be serving up tandoori lamb chops, while chocolatier Mark Tilling will be dropping by with some Halloween treats. Plus, James takes a ghostly tour of Louisiana's Houmas House

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott prepares dishes for special occasions, including chorizo-crusted hake on boulangere potatoes with salad, orange mocha mousse with cardamom, and chicken cordon bleu with cannellini mash and cherry tomatoes. He is joined in the kitchen by chef Sabrina Ghayour, who whips up marinated steak and pul biber butter, and food writer Alex Hollywood, who demonstrates her twist on pineapple and cinnamon tarte tatin

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The chef's culinary tour of Britain continues in Scotland with a trip to Blair Castle in the Highlands, where he takes a tour of the grounds and cooks venison with beetroot and blackberries in the castle's walled garden. Later, James gets a masterclass in Highland gaming as two young brothers David and Harry share the secrets to the famous caber toss

programme.title: Zookeeper (2011)
programme.desc: The animals in a zoo are able to talk, but keep this hidden from all humans. When they learn their beloved keeper is planning on leaving, the creatures decide to share their secret in the hope of persuading him to stay, and offer him some help with his unsuccessful love life. Comedy, starring Kevin James and Rosario Dawson

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Italy v England (Kick-off 4.45pm). All the action from both teams' final match of the championship, held at Stadio Olimpico. England lost to France in their opener, but then won their next three matches to maintain their chances of winning the title. They will expect to overcome the Italians, but will then have to hope that the match between France and Ireland goes in their favour. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Clive Woodward, Jonny Wilkinson and Lawrence Dallaglio, and commentary by Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and David Flatman

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking three contestants to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Su Pollard, Jordan Banjo, AJ Odudu and Jonathan Ross pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis presents a profile of this year's eight semi-finalists, ahead of the show's imminent return following a lockdown-enforced delay. Emma takes a look back at the performances of these eight contestants over the earlier rounds of this year's show, from their first Blind Audition to their Knockout Performance. Plus, the results of the Lifeline Act public vote, which will see a ninth act chosen by viewers to return to the show

programme.title: Spitting Image: US Election Special
programme.desc: The weekly puppet-based sketch show takes on the US presidential elections, with characters from both sides of the Atlantic in the firing line

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined on tonight's show by world-renowned photographer David Bailey and comedians Jack Whitehall and Roisin Conaty, while Craig David and KSI will both be performing live in the studio

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Having just turned 80, Cliff Richard joins Piers Morgan to look back on his life and six-decade career. He recalls his journey from a poverty-stricken childhood, how he became Britain's first rock-and-roll star, and at one stage the biggest selling singles artist in the world

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty hosts a quiz show in which 16 families take part in a knock out tournament as they attempt to win the holiday of a lifetime and the title of Britain's Brightest Family. In the seventh episode, the Rockliffe-King family from Cumbria and the Wright family from Durham battle for a place in the quarter-finals

programme.desc: Magoo, thinking he's at the laundromat, wanders into a candy factory, where Fizz and Weasel are creating a bubblegum that makes Fizz-shaped bubbles.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Thinking he's hunting down a rat in his attic, Magoo finds himself on the theme park, devoted to... the glory of Fizz.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo comes across Fizz encased in ice and thinks he has found a prehistoric man. He thaws him out and acts like a caveman, so as not to distress him.

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp team up to present the fun-packed show, featuring guests Bananarama, Katie Melua, Omid Djalili and Craig Revel Horwood

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Ben Fogle experiences the world beneath the Yorkshire Dales as he explores Britain's biggest underground cavern - Gaping Gill. Liz Bonnin visits Scotland's Forest Park of Galloway where she tracks one of our rarest mammals the pine martin. programme.desc: A mother of two is found murdered in her back garden. Vera and Aiden investigate the case, and delve into the disturbing secrets and ongoing conflicts that lurk beneath the surface of the victim's seemingly peaceful suburban community. The case takes a more complicated turn when the dead woman's estranged family make an unexpected reappearance, and while several of her neighbours may have had cause to carry out a crime of passion, her relatives may have had compelling motives of their own to kill. Guest starring Rita Tushingham

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott prepares dishes for special occasions, including chorizo-crusted hake on boulangere potatoes with salad, orange mocha mousse with cardamom, and chicken cordon bleu with cannellini mash and cherry tomatoes. programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The chef's culinary tour of Britain continues in Scotland with a trip to Blair Castle in the Highlands, where he takes a tour of the grounds and cooks venison with beetroot and blackberries in the castle's walled garden. Later, James gets a masterclass in Highland gaming as two young brothers David and Harry share the secrets to the famous caber toss

programme.title: Zookeeper (2011)
programme.desc: The animals in a zoo are able to talk, but keep this hidden from all humans. When they learn their beloved keeper is planning on leaving, the creatures decide to share their secret in the hope of persuading him to stay, and offer him some help with his unsuccessful love life. Comedy, starring Kevin James and Rosario Dawson

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Italy v England (Kick-off 4.45pm). All the action from both teams' final match of the championship, held at Stadio Olimpico. England lost to France in their opener, but then won their next three matches to maintain their chances of winning the title. They will expect to overcome the Italians, but will then have to hope that the match between France and Ireland goes in their favour. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Clive Woodward, Jonny Wilkinson and Lawrence Dallaglio, and commentary by Nick Mullins, Ben Kay and David Flatman

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.desc: Joel Dommett delves into the archives to bring viewers the ultimate guide every celebrity needs for surviving jungle life. With a little help from some popular ex-campmates, Joel takes viewers on a rollercoaster ride down memory lane

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Exploding toilets, airborne chainsaws and a cat with a talent for playing the drums are among the video highlights submitted by fans for comedy appraisal by Harry Hill. Plus, a prankster reveals how Star Wars villain Darth Vader takes advantage of his unique powers during an embarrassing lavatory-based situation

programme.title: Animals United (2010)
programme.desc: A lion and a meerkat go in search of water after the building of a dam cuts off their supply. On the way, they meet various other animals whose habitats have been destroyed by humans and resolve to do something about it. Animated comedy with the voices of James Corden, Stephen Fry, Jim Broadbent and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: Latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Casper (1995)
programme.desc: A paranormal expert moves into a haunted house where his daughter befriends the amiable ghost of a young boy - but things start to go awry when three smelly spooks and two dastardly crooks try to chase the new residents away. Family comedy, starring Christina Ricci, Bill Pullman, Eric Idle, Cathy Moriarty and featuring the voice of Malachi Pearson

programme.title: The Pirates! In an Adventure with Scientists! (2012)
programme.desc: The bungling leader of a band of inept buccaneers is determined to prove his worth to his rivals and win the coveted Pirate of the Year award. An encounter with the naturalist Charles Darwin gives him the chance to pull off a daring crime - if he can survive coming face to face with the pirate-hating Queen Victoria. Animated comedy, with the voices of Hugh Grant, Martin Freeman, Imelda Staunton and David Tennant

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania 2 (2015)
programme.desc: Dracula is worried that his half-human grandson is not showing any vampiric tendencies, so enlists the help of his fellow monsters in showing the youngster how it is done. The count is shocked to receive an unwelcome visit from his own father, who does not approve of his son's tolerance of humans. Animated comedy, with the voices of Adam Sandler and Andy Samberg

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: Latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania 2 (2015)
programme.desc: Dracula is worried that his half-human grandson is not showing any vampiric tendencies, so enlists the help of his fellow monsters in showing the youngster how it is done. The count is shocked to receive an unwelcome visit from his own father, who does not approve of his son's tolerance of humans. Animated comedy, with the voices of Adam Sandler and Andy Samberg

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: Latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie finally says his first word, but it is a rude one, leading to Lois being shunned by the other mums and going on a mission to find out where he learnt it

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian falls for a tech support girl in India and sets out to find her, with Stewie in tow. Meanwhile, Peter steals the show at Joe's bingo night

programme.title: Shopping with Keith Lemon
programme.desc: Keith Lemon goes shopping for sweets and science fiction memorabilia with all-round entertainer John Barrowman. He also takes presenter Emma Willis to hone her archery skills at an activity centre

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stewie and Brian form a children's band that quickly rises to fame until they let a girl get between them and drive the band apart. Meanwhile, Peter and Lois insist that Chris gets a job, so he takes a job managing Quagmire's sex life as his personal assistant

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger breaks up Steve's friendship with Snot, Barry and Toshi. Jeff realises he has a flair for house flipping and gets Stan, Francine and Hayley involved

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan hopes a beach-based assignment infiltrating a group of renegade surfers will be good for his dangerously high blood pressure. Meanwhile, Jeff and Haley have a pregnancy scare, Steve befriends a homeless drifter, and Roger tries to persuade Fifa to host the next World Cup in the Smiths' back yard. Animated comedy, with the voice of Seth MacFarlane

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Rallo tries to break up his elderly friend Murray's new romance, while Donna decides Cleveland needs to lose weight, and signs him up for a gym - but he discovers a less stressful way of getting in shape. With the guest voice of Kathryn Joosten (The West Wing)

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Junior gets a new girlfriend, but Cleveland becomes jealous of their relationship - and suspects the young woman is only trying to seduce his son so she can get permission to stay in the US. Animated comedy, with the guest voice of Rosie Perez (White Men Can't Jump)

programme.title: Hey Tracey!
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts a Halloween edition of the comedy quiz, with Bobby Norris, Rita Simons, Ellie Taylor, Darren Harriott, Jordan North and Tallia Storm trying to win some normal people £2,500 and a stay in a haunted pub

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Featuring the fastest male to run 50 metres in high heels, the most times that a car has ever been rolled, the record for most rhinestones stuck on someone's body, and the world's shortest cow

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Mike warns Deirdre she is going to lose her flat when he sells the building. Ashley gets his hair dyed blonde so he can spend time with Maxine

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre tells Ken she does not want to rush things, and he is relieved that he will not be made redundant. Roy and Bill are upset to receive notice about moving out of the flats

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tara and Kim survey the damage to the stables. Ned makes up a story about being out with poachers the previous night. Sarah tells Robert about Jack's affair

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim tells Tara that she wants to buy her out. Lisa is worried about Sam still being on the run from the police. Ned admits to Jack that he set fire to the stables

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A runaway heiress accompanies her crooked boyfriend on a series of armed robberies. But when the Sixties version of Bonnie and Clyde get into trouble they kidnap Helen, who attempts to persuade the teenager to ditch her partner in crime. Meanwhile, Peggy suggests David should take over the funeral parlour while Bernie is away. Rural drama with Sophie Ward, David Stockwell and Gwen Taylor

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The age gap between Sid and his children becomes apparent when he returns home early from work. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida gets more than she bargained for during a visit to the fish and chip shop

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective faces a tough time while looking into a murder at a peaceful college. After suffering the familiar pangs of unrequited love, he clashes with the unpleasant college master and then unwittingly provides his main suspects with a watertight alibi. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Robert Stephens, Amanda Burton and Philip Middlemiss

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police are called in to investigate when a local judge is the target of a series of mysterious attacks, but the case takes a sinister turn when the prime suspect is found dead on his own doorstep. Peggy is delighted when David's new radio starts picking up police broadcasts, but a news reporter doesn't share her enthusiasm. Rural drama, with Gwen Taylor and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned is angry with Jan for forcing Jack to leave. Kim invites Sophie to come with her to New Zealand. Zoe tells Sophie that if she leaves she need not come back

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sophie tells Zoe she has changed her mind about not wanting a baby with her. Sam decides to give himself up to the police. Viv flirts with Paddy when they get drunk together

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen knows Bill is to be evicted and wonders why he has not mentioned it. Roy surprises Gail and Martin by agreeing to buy the cafe. Judy discovers she is not pregnant

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Judy tells Gary she is not pregnant and he wonders if he is sterile. Deirdre feels Ken is taking it for granted that she will move in with him

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the apparent suicide of a solicitor rumoured to have been stealing money from clients. When rival lawyer Otto Benham is later found murdered in a bizarre manner, the detectives begin to suspect the two deaths may be related. Mystery, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Barry Jackson, James Weber Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective faces a tough time while looking into a murder at a peaceful college. After suffering the familiar pangs of unrequited love, he clashes with the unpleasant college master and then unwittingly provides his main suspects with a watertight alibi. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Robert Stephens, Amanda Burton and Philip Middlemiss

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Penhale decides to have a Police Open day to raise community awareness, but the only genuine visitor is an angry farmer who has had three of her sheep stolen. Meanwhile, Morwenna decides to take Al away for the weekend to try out Hannah's new glamping site, but the trip does not go well as Al accidently burns down the yurt. Elsewhere, James has started biting not only Louisa but other children at the nursery. Louisa speaks to Tina about it, but is surprised to find out Tina has spoken to their lecturer about it too

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: It is the annual Portwenn versus Port Carran gig race and school gig festival, and this time Penhale is the umpire and Morwenna is the Portwenn team captain. However, Morwenna is forced to find a replacement rower when Eric dislocates his finger. Meanwhile, Mel Hendy goes to see Martin at the surgery worried about a rash that could be infectious, but she ends up opening up to him about her concerns over her husband's drinking. Elsewhere, Martin and Louisa arrive home to find Chris Parsons waiting for them

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An American businessman fears his life is in danger while travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris, and offers fellow passenger Poirot $10,000 for protection. After rejecting the proposal, the sleuth wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow in Serbia. Racing against time to find the killer among the passengers, Poirot must look to the past to solve the crime, and is faced with a decision that will haunt him for ever. David Suchet takes the title role in the murder mystery, with guest stars Hugh Bonneville, David Morrissey and Eileen Atkins

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The badly beaten body of 18-year-old clerk Sylvia Kane is found in a pub car park by her boyfriend John Sanders. Called in to investigate, Morse finds a coded letter in her bag and sets about trying to decipher it, gradually unravelling a tangled web of hidden passion and corruption at the insurance office where she worked. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Anthony Bate, Terrence Hardiman, Jill Baker, Holly Aird and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist's wife is murdered during an excavation in the Middle East. Fortunately, a vacationing Poirot - who shares with most great detectives an inability to go on holiday without stumbling over a body or two - is on hand to investigate. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Ron Berglas, Georgina Sowerby and Iain Mitchell

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. A masked Scott bursts into the bistro and threatens everyone with a gun, while Steve breaks the news to Leanne that he's tried 10 more specialists but no one is willing to fight their corner. Elsewhere, Imran suggests to Alya they might have to sell Yasmeen's house to pay her legal fees

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate crime and mayhem in the countryside as they take on a murder case in picturesque Fletcher's Cross. Undertaker Patrick Pennyman is found dead in his funeral parlour and the detectives suspect a possible link to the local spiritualist church. When another body turns up, the pressure to find the culprit redoubles. John Nettles, John Hopkins and Jane Wymark star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when ladies' man and rowing club member Guy Sweetman is found dead in the river during the annual Midsomer Regatta. They soon discover the elite exterior of the club hides a world of money problems and sexual jealousy and the victim had a number of enemies who might have wanted him dead. Guest starring Olympic rower Steve Redgrave and Diana Quick

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott enter the world of botany to search for the killer of a retired teacher found dead shortly after smuggling a rare orchid out of Borneo. The discovery of two more bodies makes the detectives realise the murderer will stop at nothing to get hold of the coveted plant, and it seems the key to the mystery may lie in a Latin notebook. John Nettles and John Hopkins star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual harvest fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death arrive in the Midsomer village of Whitcombe Mallet. But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Mystery drama set in Bath, where the wildly ambitious DCI McDonald finds herself paired with the shy, modest DS Dodds, who has languished on the shelf for most of his working life. However, it soon turns out the unassuming sergeant has a hidden talent for puzzle solving. Their first case sees the pair looking into the shooting of a homeless man in the deserted mansion of local industrialist Max Crockett. The Crockett appear to be a close-knit group but it soon becomes clear they have secrets to hide. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star as the detectives, with a guest appearance from Robert Lindsay

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Michael Kitchen returns to the role of Christopher Foyle, senior intelligence officer for MI5, in the first of three films. Foyle is drawn into the world of corrupt Nazi businessmen when a university professor is found murdered in a London park. When it transpires the victim was working as a translator in Nuremberg, the detective realises there are powerful people trying to conceal secrets from the Second World War. Meanwhile, Sam (Honeysuckle Weeks) volunteers for a risky undercover job, despite being pregnant. Guest stars include John Mahoney (Frasier), Jaime Winstone (Mad Dogs) and Nigel Lindsay (Four Lions)

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: Adaptation of William Makepeace Thackeray's novel about an amoral woman who cunningly manipulates her way to the top of Georgian high society, while the Napoleonic wars rage around her. Starring Olivia Cooke and Claudia Jessie, with Michael Palin appearing as Thackeray himself

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid suffers temporary amnesia, but Jean is on hand to produce reminders of his antics at the annual Boy Scouts reunion. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike attempts to earn some cash without Sid's interference. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the North Yorkshire coastline as he investigates the nation's passion for being beside the sea, beginning in the historic town of Scarborough and travelling to the port of Whitby - where he goes on a ghostly adventure

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the dramatic and beautiful landscape of the Lake District. Along the way he meets the man who made the world's longest Cumberland sausage, attempts to speed along the steepest road in Britain, crosses the Infinity Bridge and tries to break the record for the slowest water ski in history

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. A masked Scott bursts into the bistro and threatens everyone with a gun, while Steve breaks the news to Leanne that he's tried 10 more specialists but no one is willing to fight their corner. Elsewhere, Imran suggests to Alya they might have to sell Yasmeen's house to pay her legal fees

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: Carry On Dick (1974)
programme.desc: Two officers of the law dedicated to catching a cheeky highwayman enlist the help of a vicar, unaware the pious pastor and his buxom housemaid are not as innocent as they seem to be. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Jack Douglas, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Hattie Jacques

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when a man collapses and dies outside a pub after a fight with his brother-in-law. The discovery of suspicious burn marks on the victim's forehead reveals sinister secrets and when a second body is found, the detectives are drawn into a world of superstition and family tensions. Murder mystery with John Nettles, John Hopkins and guest starring Geoffrey Whitehead

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the apparent suicide of a solicitor rumoured to have been stealing money from clients. When rival lawyer Otto Benham is later found murdered in a bizarre manner, the detectives begin to suspect the two deaths may be related. Mystery, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Barry Jackson, James Weber Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Schoolteacher Connie Bishop keeps waking in the night, convinced she has an intruder, but most of the villagers in Badger's Drift dismiss her fears as an overactive imagination. Days later, a local councillor thought to have been obsessed with her is found murdered in her garden, and the ensuing investigation by Barnaby and Jones uncovers jealous rivals, ruthless social climbers and a cottage with a few secrets. Guest starring Paul Kaye, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: Anne and James are at loggerheads following the revelations at the Brockenhursts' party. With Oliver fixated on discovering the origins of his father's new protege, Susan uses the opportunity to plan a covert trip to meet with a new friend, and enlists her maid Speer to keep the true nature of the rendezvous under wraps. Meanwhile, Lady Templemore begins work on her lavish plans for her daughter's wedding, but much to her disappointment, Lady Maria seems fixated on someone other than her fiance

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: John appeals to members of the Trenchards' staff in his bid to uncover the truth about Charles, while Turton and Ellis are quick to betray their employers and prove surprisingly adept at deceit and spying. Susan makes a seemingly miraculous discovery, and begrudgingly agrees to accompany Anne and James to their country estate in the hope of extracting further information about Oliver. Stephen makes a last-ditch attempt to secure the funds he needs to pay off his debt - a deal that puts his life in danger

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: The search for a serial killer in the area around Loch Ness becomes a matter of life and death for local detective Annie Redford, who is trying to cope with her first murder case. When the body of a local man is found at the foot of Carn Mohr Mountain and an isolated human heart on the loch shore, the town's normality is shattered and the nightmare begins. Thriller, starring Laura Fraser and Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The last run of the detective drama ended with Lewis (Kevin Whately) starting a new life away from the force, but it seems that peace and quiet doesn't suit him - which is just as well, as newly promoted DI Hathaway (Laurence Fox) is struggling to find a sidekick, and within just four weeks is already on to his second sergeant, DS Lizzie Maddox (Angela Griffin). So, Chief Superintendent Innocent (Rebecca Front) decides to reunite the former partners to look into the murder of a neurosurgeon, a case with potential links to the worlds of animal rights and blood sports. In the first of this two-part story, suspicion falls on glamorous widow Erica (Kara Tointon), but it turns out there are plenty of other people in the victim's inner circle who were driven by fear and loathing

programme.title: The Royal Wives of Windsor
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ahead of the wedding of Prince Harry and Meghan Markle, this documentary explores how the women who have married into the house of Windsor over the last century have transformed one of the most famous dynasties in the world. Historians including David Starkey, Kate Williams and Piers Brendon, broadcasters Jeremy Paxman and Gyles Brandreth, biographers Andrew Morton, Penny Junor and Anne Sebba, royal insiders and journalists provide a review of British social history as reflected through the lives of the royal wives of Windsor

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The family return from holiday, and it seems as if they never went away. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist's wife is murdered during an excavation in the Middle East. Fortunately, a vacationing Poirot - who shares with most great detectives an inability to go on holiday without stumbling over a body or two - is on hand to investigate. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Ron Berglas, Georgina Sowerby and Iain Mitchell

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. A masked Scott bursts into the bistro and threatens everyone with a gun, while Steve breaks the news to Leanne that he's tried 10 more specialists but no one is willing to fight their corner. Elsewhere, Imran suggests to Alya they might have to sell Yasmeen's house to pay her legal fees

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate crime and mayhem in the countryside as they take on a murder case in picturesque Fletcher's Cross. Undertaker Patrick Pennyman is found dead in his funeral parlour and the detectives suspect a possible link to the local spiritualist church. When another body turns up, the pressure to find the culprit redoubles. John Nettles, John Hopkins and Jane Wymark star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when ladies' man and rowing club member Guy Sweetman is found dead in the river during the annual Midsomer Regatta. They soon discover the elite exterior of the club hides a world of money problems and sexual jealousy and the victim had a number of enemies who might have wanted him dead. Guest starring Olympic rower Steve Redgrave and Diana Quick

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott enter the world of botany to search for the killer of a retired teacher found dead shortly after smuggling a rare orchid out of Borneo. The discovery of two more bodies makes the detectives realise the murderer will stop at nothing to get hold of the coveted plant, and it seems the key to the mystery may lie in a Latin notebook. John Nettles and John Hopkins star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A guest at a village Halloween party boasts of having witnessed a murder years previously - and later meets a grisly end in keeping with the macabre spirit of the occasion. Arriving to investigate, Poirot delves back into the annals of local life - and uncovers a series of mysterious deaths, any one of which could have a bearing on the present case. David Suchet, Amelia Bullmore and Zoe Wanamaker star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual harvest fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death arrive in the Midsomer village of Whitcombe Mallet. But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Mystery drama set in Bath, where the wildly ambitious DCI McDonald finds herself paired with the shy, modest DS Dodds, who has languished on the shelf for most of his working life. However, it soon turns out the unassuming sergeant has a hidden talent for puzzle solving. Their first case sees the pair looking into the shooting of a homeless man in the deserted mansion of local industrialist Max Crockett. The Crockett appear to be a close-knit group but it soon becomes clear they have secrets to hide. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star as the detectives, with a guest appearance from Robert Lindsay

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Michael Kitchen returns to the role of Christopher Foyle, senior intelligence officer for MI5, in the first of three films. Foyle is drawn into the world of corrupt Nazi businessmen when a university professor is found murdered in a London park. When it transpires the victim was working as a translator in Nuremberg, the detective realises there are powerful people trying to conceal secrets from the Second World War. Meanwhile, Sam (Honeysuckle Weeks) volunteers for a risky undercover job, despite being pregnant. Guest stars include John Mahoney (Frasier), Jaime Winstone (Mad Dogs) and Nigel Lindsay (Four Lions)

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: Adaptation of William Makepeace Thackeray's novel about an amoral woman who cunningly manipulates her way to the top of Georgian high society, while the Napoleonic wars rage around her. Starring Olivia Cooke and Claudia Jessie, with Michael Palin appearing as Thackeray himself

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid suffers temporary amnesia, but Jean is on hand to produce reminders of his antics at the annual Boy Scouts reunion. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike attempts to earn some cash without Sid's interference. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the North Yorkshire coastline as he investigates the nation's passion for being beside the sea, beginning in the historic town of Scarborough and travelling to the port of Whitby - where he goes on a ghostly adventure

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the dramatic and beautiful landscape of the Lake District. Along the way he meets the man who made the world's longest Cumberland sausage, attempts to speed along the steepest road in Britain, crosses the Infinity Bridge and tries to break the record for the slowest water ski in history

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Elsewhere, Imran suggests to Alya they might have to sell Yasmeen's house to pay her legal fees

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: Carry On Dick (1974)
programme.desc: Two officers of the law dedicated to catching a cheeky highwayman enlist the help of a vicar, unaware the pious pastor and his buxom housemaid are not as innocent as they seem to be. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Jack Douglas, Barbara Windsor, Bernard Bresslaw, Joan Sims and Hattie Jacques

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when a man collapses and dies outside a pub after a fight with his brother-in-law. The discovery of suspicious burn marks on the victim's forehead reveals sinister secrets and when a second body is found, the detectives are drawn into a world of superstition and family tensions. Murder mystery with John Nettles, John Hopkins and guest starring Geoffrey Whitehead

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the apparent suicide of a solicitor rumoured to have been stealing money from clients. When rival lawyer Otto Benham is later found murdered in a bizarre manner, the detectives begin to suspect the two deaths may be related. Mystery, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Barry Jackson, James Weber Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Schoolteacher Connie Bishop keeps waking in the night, convinced she has an intruder, but most of the villagers in Badger's Drift dismiss her fears as an overactive imagination. Days later, a local councillor thought to have been obsessed with her is found murdered in her garden, and the ensuing investigation by Barnaby and Jones uncovers jealous rivals, ruthless social climbers and a cottage with a few secrets. Guest starring Paul Kaye, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: Anne and James are at loggerheads following the revelations at the Brockenhursts' party. With Oliver fixated on discovering the origins of his father's new protege, Susan uses the opportunity to plan a covert trip to meet with a new friend, and enlists her maid Speer to keep the true nature of the rendezvous under wraps. Meanwhile, Lady Templemore begins work on her lavish plans for her daughter's wedding, but much to her disappointment, Lady Maria seems fixated on someone other than her fiance

programme.title: Belgravia
programme.desc: John appeals to members of the Trenchards' staff in his bid to uncover the truth about Charles, while Turton and Ellis are quick to betray their employers and prove surprisingly adept at deceit and spying. Susan makes a seemingly miraculous discovery, and begrudgingly agrees to accompany Anne and James to their country estate in the hope of extracting further information about Oliver. Stephen makes a last-ditch attempt to secure the funds he needs to pay off his debt - a deal that puts his life in danger

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: The search for a serial killer in the area around Loch Ness becomes a matter of life and death for local detective Annie Redford, who is trying to cope with her first murder case. When the body of a local man is found at the foot of Carn Mohr Mountain and an isolated human heart on the loch shore, the town's normality is shattered and the nightmare begins. Thriller, starring Laura Fraser and Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The last run of the detective drama ended with Lewis (Kevin Whately) starting a new life away from the force, but it seems that peace and quiet doesn't suit him - which is just as well, as newly promoted DI Hathaway (Laurence Fox) is struggling to find a sidekick, and within just four weeks is already on to his second sergeant, DS Lizzie Maddox (Angela Griffin). So, Chief Superintendent Innocent (Rebecca Front) decides to reunite the former partners to look into the murder of a neurosurgeon, a case with potential links to the worlds of animal rights and blood sports. In the first of this two-part story, suspicion falls on glamorous widow Erica (Kara Tointon), but it turns out there are plenty of other people in the victim's inner circle who were driven by fear and loathing

programme.title: The Royal Wives of Windsor
programme.desc: Part one of two. Ahead of the wedding of Prince Harry and Meghan Markle, this documentary explores how the women who have married into the house of Windsor over the last century have transformed one of the most famous dynasties in the world. Historians including David Starkey, Kate Williams and Piers Brendon, broadcasters Jeremy Paxman and Gyles Brandreth, biographers Andrew Morton, Penny Junor and Anne Sebba, royal insiders and journalists provide a review of British social history as reflected through the lives of the royal wives of Windsor

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The family return from holiday, and it seems as if they never went away. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar investigates the suspicious deaths of five company directors, who each received a card with the words 'The Scales of Justice' printed on it prior to their demise. However, he finds his inquiries frustrated by the remaining members of the board. Roger Moore stars

programme.title: Robin of Sherwood
programme.desc: The outlaws are attacked by a group of angry Crusaders who believe Robin is a thief. Michael Praed stars

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Mother spends his birthday telling his elderly aunts a tall tale about a criminal plot to steal art treasures from a high security vault. The duo get all excited over the dastardly plot to steal the Crown Jewels

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A young widow is swindled out of her husband's insurance money by con artist Freddie Fenton (Derek Jacobi), so Terry responds by holding the crook's beloved Rolls Royce hostage. Comedy drama, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle infiltrate a gang smuggling art treasures and industrial secrets out of the country and selling them on to foreign powers. As the investigation proceeds, the duo discover the illicit operation is being run by a country squire in his stately home. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.desc: A gambler is robbed of his winnings and calls on Arthur and Terry to help him track the money down. Comedy drama, guest starring Anthony Valentine and featuring an early appearance by Marina Sirtis (Star Trek: The Next Generation). With George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A recently released convict causes chaos when he takes a group of hospital patients hostage. Bodie and Doyle try to get to him before he can start shooting. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of this afternoon's racing, featuring the 2.25 Champion Chase from Down Royal, the 2.45 West Yorkshire Hurdle and races at 1.35, 2.10 and 3.20 from Wetherby, and the 3.40 Sodexo Gold Cup and races at 1.20 and 3.05 from Ascot. With analysis from Luke Harvey and Mick Fitzgerald, reports from Oli Bell and Matt Chapman, and commentary by Mark Johnson

programme.title: Bend of the River (1952)
programme.desc: A trail guide saves a thief from being lynched and recruits him to help guide a party of settlers across the country. Once the homesteaders have reached their destination, the two men agree to go for supplies, but on the way back, the thief plots to sell their goods to a gold-mining camp. Western, starring James Stewart, Arthur Kennedy, Rock Hudson, Lori Nelson, Jay C Flippen and Julie Adams

programme.title: European Cup Winners: Nottingham Forest
programme.desc: Between 1977 and 1984 English football teams dominated the European Cup and Nottingham Forest, under charismatic manager Brian Clough, lifted the famous trophy twice. Gabriel Clarke looks at how Clough and his assistant Peter Taylor built a championship-winning team and then shocked the world of football by dominating its premier club competition two years in a row. Featuring highlights of both of those finals and interviews with some of the key individuals involved

programme.title: Bend of the River (1952)

programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Laura Hamilton help a couple to choose between a property on the western coastline of Wales and a home in Carcassonne in the south of France

programme.title: The Great House Giveaway
programme.desc: Strangers Harry and Kaylum buy a majestic detached house in Ashton-under-Lyne in Greater Manchester at auction - then have six months to renovate and sell it on for a profit. Can they put their differences aside and turn this property around?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet up for a final showdown, as Kaye and Lily bring Harriet and Richard's blind issues to light before dishing the dirt on Sophie and David's discoveries. Amanda wants to know why frank feelings were unleased when it came to her canine companion, Trudy. David and Sophie banish Harriet and Richard's bathroom blues, before confronting Kaye and Lily with their cleanliness critique. After opinions are aired and differences debated, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: t's the final night in Edinburgh, hosted by food and fashion-conscious Bruce. All week Bruce has expressed his desire for the finer things in life and his dislike of anything that falls below his high standards, so expectations are high. Can Bruce pull the evening off with style, and will he do enough to grab the cash?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge has plastic surgery in an effort to lose weight, but a mix-up results in her being given breast implants instead - much to Homer's delight. With the guest voices of Adam West and Burt Ward, stars of 1960s TV series Batman

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart and his family enter the twilight zone in three more cartoon tales of horror, including the discovery of Bart's demented twin in the attic and Lisa being the god to an alternative human species cultured in a margarine tub. Plus, Homer tries to expose two fraudulent presidential candidates

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off: An Extra Slice
programme.desc: Jo Brand is joined by Jonathan Ross, Darren Harriott and Siobhan McSweeney to discuss all the action from a Bake Off first - Japanese Week - featuring an interview with the eliminated contestant. Tom Allen puts a select group of home bakers through their paces from a safe distance, and Jo showcases more of the weird and wonderful delights from viewers' kitchens

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shows including This Morning, The Million Pound Cube, Strictly Come Dancing, The Undoing, The Yorkshire Vet, First Dates, BBC news reports on the US election and a BBC Breakfast report on the Welsh shopping ban

programme.title: The Last Leg
programme.desc: Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker are joined by celebrity guests Sandi Toksvig and Desiree Burch for a comic review of the significant moments of the past seven days. As usual, the hashtag isitok paves the way for the gang to round up, examine and explain the leading and most entertaining news stories of the week

programme.title: Random Acts
programme.desc: Black film-maker Denise Alder brings West African art alive in Mablo Micasso's Dream, a surreal and beautiful journey through culture and myth. This is showing as part of Channel 4's programming for Black History Month, which offers an important platform for different aspects of the Black experience in modern Britain

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A look back at a host of classic dates from the show, where eyes met, sparks flew, and love truly blossomed. Dapper gent Will is set up on a date with fellow tweed admirer Fran, while Bristol Rastafarian and single mother Ibiba meets fellow Bristolian Aarron. Elsewhere, Octogenarian Doreen meets 86-year-old author James, who is more than ready to write the next chapter of his love life

programme.title: The Witch (2015)
programme.desc: An English farmer is driven from his land in 17th-century America, and settles on the edge of a sinister forest. The family suffers a series of inexplicable disasters, and as tension rises between them, the eldest daughter finds herself accused by her own parents and siblings of being a witch. Period horror, starring Anya Taylor-Joy and Ralph Ineson

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Promotions representative Dawn Griffiths hosts the first dinner party in Middlesbrough, and has confidence in her Asian-themed evening of curry and traditional Indian dancing. However, her rowdy guests - practical joker Ron Gordon, market trader Kay Griffiths, teacher Karena Serdecka and refuse collector Paul Tidy - prove hard to please

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Practical joker Ron Gordon hosts the second party in Middlesbrough, but despite his expertly cooked food, his hopes of an evening of elegance are placed in jeopardy by market trader Kay Griffiths, who dominated proceedings the previous night, but irritated her fellow diners

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Self-confessed loudmouth Kay Griffiths hosts the third dinner party in Middlesbrough, hoping to liven up her guests with good food, colourful conversation, a little conflict and her home-made punch

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Drama teacher Karena Serdecka hosts the fourth dinner party in Middlesbrough, treating her guests to a Dutch and Polish menu, before taking to the stage to sing with her band

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Refuse collector Paul Tidy hosts the final evening in Middlesbrough, throwing a film star-themed party in the hope of impressing his guests. His plans take a turn for the worse when one of his diners expresses a dislike of garlic, which happens to be one of the chef's favourite ingredients, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Food Unwrapped Investigates
programme.desc: Kate Quilton uncovers the truth behind some of Britain's most shocking food headlines, meeting the case studies behind the stories as well as exploring the science, conducting experiments and questioning those in control. She begins by investigating the world of severe food allergies, enlisting the help of a team of sufferers to report undercover at a host of different London eateries to see how they are treated

programme.title: Fifteen to One
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts the general knowledge quiz in which 15 contestants choose whether to take the question or nominate in the hope of making it through to the end-of-series final

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The bus crosses the border into Slovenia, where Brendan encourages the tourists to take part in a few water sports - followed by a sticky honey massage at a bee farm

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy host a Valentine's Day episode of Fun with Flags, while Leonard and Penny grapple with getting older. Elsewhere, Howard and Bernadette discover a surprise in their hot tub, and Raj is torn between Emily and a girl he met at the Comic Bookstore. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
